event_blocks.summary: Futuristic synth music and whooshing sounds accompanied the title 'Young Justice: Outsiders – Season 3 Crash Course,' transitioning to a cosmic backdrop of Earth and Mars, noting two years have passed since the team and League vanquished the Reach. A growing team with new leaders was depicted in a starry space setting. Kaldur, the Bald Man in Orange and Green Suit, calmly addressed the Superman T-Shirt Man, identified as Aquaman, and invited "Megan" to observe as leader, while text declared "Meta-humans continue to be targets of villainous forces." A woman in a purple and blue armored suit with a glowing blue aura spoke with a clear, serious, and authoritative female voice. A young man's eye glowed red, followed by the emergence of a Fiery Red-Veined Figure from flames, a sleek, dark spaceship with glowing orange engines flying through space, and another red, fiery figure leaping through green smoke. In a high-tech room with a futuristic, glowing green holographic display, Kaldur stated, "Let us get right to it. My co-chair has urgent news from the heroes she is leading on our various missions in space." The Woman in Purple and Blue Armored Suit confirmed, "All right, we have confirmation. Metahuman trafficking on Earth has spilled out into the galaxy. On multiple worlds, Earth's metahumans are being deployed by the enemy as weapons of mass destruction." Kaldur began, "Here on Earth, our ongoing struggle with metahuman trafficking has..." before being interrupted by a deeper, gravelly male voice saying "struggle," and another slightly higher-pitched, urgent male voice adding "tried disaster." A council of heroes, including the Blonde Woman in Dark Jacket, the Hooded Archer, and the Man in Red Suit with Lightning Bolt, appeared serious and focused. The Blonde Woman in Dark Jacket explained, "Teens and even kids are being abducted on an unprecedented scale. They're tested for the metagene and if they..." Those who "test positive" are "used as guinea pigs to create new metahumans," according to the Blonde Woman in Dark Jacket, while the Bald Man in Orange and Green Suit added that those who "test negative, they're rarely seen again." The Hooded Archer declared that "metahumans are being treated as the next exploitable resource" by nations and corporations, calling it a "global pandemic." Batman, flanked by the Blonde Woman in Dark Jacket and the Man in Red Suit with Lightning Bolt, was addressed by the Bald Man in Orange and Green Suit, who emphasized that "globally, we are faced with obstacles at every turn," and that "United Nations Secretary General Lex Luthor is using our UN charter to place more and more restrictions on the league." The Superman T-Shirt Man lamented that "we've become removed, distant from the people the league was created to serve." The Hooded Archer suggested, "Perhaps it's time to acknowledge that the league has outlived its usefulness on Earth," prompting the Bald Man in Orange and Green Suit to exclaim, "He's hamstrung us!" The Masked Figure in Black observed the council from a distance. The Blonde Woman in Dark Jacket challenged Batman, "that's overstating things, don't you think?" Batman responded, "No, I don't. And I'm offering an alternative." The Bald Man in Orange and Green Suit countered, "If your alternative is to disband the league, I think you are forgetting all of the good we do." Batman sharply interjected, "Did. Past tense." The Bald Man in Orange and Green Suit concluded, "Present tense. The league has its difficulties," as the council members listened intently in the dark, high-tech environment.

Blonde Woman in Dark Jacket.description: A woman with blonde hair, wearing a dark jacket over a black top, accessorized with a choker; she appears stern and commanding in a council setting, seated beside Superman T-Shirt Man, and speaks with a distinct female voice, discussing metahumans and later challenging Batman's statements.

Woman in Purple and Blue Armored Suit.description: A woman with dark hair, wearing a purple and blue armored suit with a star emblem on the chest, glowing with a blue aura, and holding a sword; she exudes confidence and readiness, appearing as a hologram beside the Bald Man in Orange and Green Suit.

Man in Red Suit with Lightning Bolt.description: A man in a red suit with a yellow lightning bolt emblem on the chest, seated at the council table, appearing strong and authoritative; he speaks with a distinct male voice, lamenting that the league has 'become removed' from the people it serves.

Spaceship.description: A sleek, dark spaceship with glowing orange engines, flying through space, suggesting a mission or pursuit.

Bald Man in Orange and Green Suit.description: A bald man wearing a form-fitting suit with orange torso and green arms, featuring black accents and a V-shaped emblem on the chest; he appears serious and authoritative, speaking with an intense, commanding male voice, at one point shouting 'He's hamstrung us!' and later engaging in a sharp debate with Batman.

Fiery Red-Veined Figure.description: A humanoid figure engulfed in red flames with glowing red veins, suggesting a powerful, possibly corrupted or transformed state.

Superman T-Shirt Man.description: A man with dark hair wearing a black t-shirt with a red Superman 'S' emblem, standing beside the bald man in the orange suit in a space setting.

Young Man with Glowing Red Eye.description: A young man with green eyes, shown in close-up, whose eye suddenly glows red, indicating a transformation or power activation.

Holographic Display.description: A futuristic, glowing green hologram in the background, showing a Y-shaped structure, indicating advanced technology in the setting.

Masked Figure in Black.description: A figure in a black hooded outfit with a red emblem on the chest, standing among the team in space, partially obscured, now seen in a dark blue hooded cloak, observing the council from a distance.

Hooded Archer.description: A muscular man in a green hooded outfit with arrows on his back, seated at a council table, looking serious and focused; he speaks with a distinct male voice, discussing metahumans and suggesting the league has 'outlived its usefulness'.

Batman.description: A figure in a black armored suit with a bat emblem, standing with arms crossed, displaying a stern and commanding presence; he speaks with a deep, firm, and resolute male voice, offering an 'alternative' and sharply correcting the Bald Man in Orange and Green Suit with 'Did. Past tense'.

event_blocks.summary: In a dimly lit, futuristic council chamber, Batman, flanked by the Blonde Woman in Dark Jacket and the Hooded Archer, witnesses the Hooded Archer and another member tender their resignations from the Justice League, confirmed by holographic displays and spoken regrets. A bearded man expresses distrust in Batman Incorporated, prompting a grim reaction from Batman. The scene shifts to a space environment with the Young Man with Glowing Red Eye, the Masked Figure in Black, and the Blonde Woman in Dark Jacket, while the bearded man's determination is noted. The League decides to issue a statement disowning the former members to avoid responsibility. Simultaneously, at the United Nations Meta-Human Summit, Lex Luthor, as U.N. Secretary General, addresses the disappointment of world nations regarding certain leaguers. In a separate event, Nightwing investigates a meta-human trafficking lab in a warehouse, discovering containment pods filled with 'Tar' and identifying Markovia as the origin, vowing to go there. This coincides with a broadcast from Markovia, where King Viktor and Queen Ilona pledge their nation's resources to end child abductions.

raw_segments: The minute opens with a tense council meeting in a dimly lit, futuristic chamber, where Batman stands sternly with arms crossed, flanked by the Blonde Woman in Dark Jacket and the Hooded Archer, all displaying serious expressions. A male voice states, "I acknowledge that," followed by the Hooded Archer's firm declaration, "I'm sorry, but I hereby tender my resignation to the Justice League." Tense, dramatic music swells as another male voice adds, "So do I," suggesting a second resignation. The scene shifts to Superman T-Shirt Man seated beside the Man in Red Suit with Lightning Bolt, both appearing concerned, while a distressed female voice urgently calls out, "Ollie!" confirming the Hooded Archer's identity. Electronic beeping and system alerts accompany a holographic display showing five figures, two marked with glowing 'RESIGN' signs, visually confirming the resignations. A weary male voice states, "Can't do this anymore," followed by a regretful "I'm sorry," and an understanding "I understand." A deep, authoritative voice declares, "It's time," prompting a female voice to question, "Time? Time for what?" An automated female voice announces, "recognize Green Arrow. Zero," further identifying the Hooded Archer. The distressed female voice returns, asking, "Robin, Tim, Spoiler, where are you going?" as the automated voice continues with "Arrowette, B two seven. Robin, B." The focus then moves to a close-up of a man with a beard and dark hair, who speaks with intensity, stating, "I want nothing to do with Batman Incorporated. The truth is, I don't trust you." Batman turns sharply, his expression grim, reacting to these defiant words. The setting transitions to a starry space environment, where a group of heroes, including the Young Man with Glowing Red Eye, the Masked Figure in Black, and the Blonde Woman in Dark Jacket, stand on a platform. The Masked Figure in Black walks forward, while Superman T-Shirt Man stands alone, observing. The camera then focuses on the bearded man again, now in a space setting, his face filled with determination. A calm, official male voice states, "We'll have to issue a statement," to which a sharp female voice asks, "You mean disown them?" The official voice confirms, "Yes. So the League will not be held responsible for its former members' actions." The scene shifts back to the council, where the Bald Man in Orange and Green Suit and the Woman in Purple and Blue Armored Suit appear, the latter glowing with a blue aura. The final sequence shows a live broadcast from the United Nations Meta-Human Summit, with Lex Luthor, identified as U.N. Secretary General, speaking at a podium, flanked by large screens displaying his face, under the Galaxy Broadcasting System logo. Lex Luthor's formal voice expresses, "It goes without saying that the independent nations of the world are deeply disappointed that some leaguers..." leaving the atmosphere charged with political tension and impending decisions.

The video opens at the United Nations Meta-Human Summit, where Garth, Ambassador of Atlantis, stands solemnly in a dark blue armored suit, his deep, authoritative voice declaring that some "don't seem to believe in the values their league claims to represent." A different, agitated male voice then interjects, blaming the Justice League and stating that "these policies are costing lives." The scene transitions through a 'NIGHTWING INVESTIGATES THE META-HUMAN TRAFFICKING' title card to a dimly lit warehouse. Here, Nightwing, in his black and blue tactical suit, observes two young individuals emerging from glowing containment pods amidst metallic clangs and high-tech scanning sounds. He uses his high-tech gauntlet to scan a device, projecting holographic data, and states, "It's definitely another meta-human trafficking lab." A female voice asks if it's "Bedlam," and Nightwing confirms seeing "pods full of black goo." The female voice explains that "Bedlam uses a substance called Tar as a catalyst" as the holographic scan reveals a flag with a golden eagle and a red, white, and green tricolor. Nightwing identifies the origin: "Tar contains a clay only found in one place... Markovia," then declares, "Within Markovia, here we come." The scene cuts to a live broadcast labeled 'King & Queen Concerned Over Child Abductions,' showing Queen Ilona in a light blue dress waving, and King Viktor in a red military uniform on a balcony. King Viktor's deep, regal voice pledges "all of Markovia's resources to see that scourge ended," before a female announcer identifies them as "Markovia's King Viktor Markov and Queen..."

event_blocks.summary: Cat Grant reported from Markovburg on the second anniversary of Princess Tara's abduction, noting the lack of ransom and her unexplained disappearance. In a grand hall, Prince Brion Markov learned from a woman in a white lab coat that he tested positive for the metagene, confirming Princess Tara likely would have too, explaining her abduction. A title card announced 'NIGHTWING'S COVERT TEAM INVESTIGATES AFTER META-HUMAN TRAFFICKERS STRIKE IN MARKOVIA.' Subsequently, a man in a white t-shirt, exhibiting glowing red veins, was violently thrown through a door. Crown Prince Gregor Markov, in a red military uniform, announced the assassination of King Viktor and Queen Ilona. A voiceover identified 'Bedlam' as a notorious meta-human trafficking syndicate suspected of the assassinations, with potential connections to further events. Later, at a nighttime outdoor gathering, holographic interfaces identified geneticist Ecks, Simon and Baron Frederick Delamb. The voiceover described Simon X as a renowned geneticist and Frederick Delamb as the late Queen's brother and potential regent. Prince Brion, in a tuxedo, questioned by a blonde woman about activating his metagene, stated daring action was needed to save Markovia, and she directed him to Dr. Simon X. A title card declared 'PRINCE BRION TAKES MATTERS INTO HIS OWN HANDS TO AVENGE HIS FAMILY AND HIS COUNTRY.' In an industrial interior, Brion confronted Simon Ecks, who discussed metagene potential. Brion accused Ecks of being a trafficker stealing sick children from a hospital, to which Ecks responded, "They're not all sick." Brion demanded to know how many children had been taken and murdered, as a third man in a green coat with a gold medallion observed, and the scene devolved into sounds of impact, struggle, and pain.

She speaks with a calm, analytical female voice, questioning Brion's 'metagene quest' and later directing him to Dr. Simon X.

He speaks with a sophisticated, slightly condescending male voice, discussing 'metagene potential' and later calmly dismisses Brion's accusations, stating, 'They're not all sick.'

raw_segments: The video opens with a live news broadcast from Galaxy Broadcasting System, where Cat Grant, in a purple blazer, reports from Markovburg, Markovia, against a desert backdrop and statue. She announces that King Viktor and Queen Ilona DeLam Markov are holding a press conference on the second anniversary of their daughter Princess Tara's abduction, noting no ransom was made and she simply vanished. The scene transitions to a grand hall where a man, identified as Prince Brion Markov, in a dark coat and brown vest, is in a tense conversation with a woman in a white lab coat and glasses. The woman asks "Prince Brion?" and he inquires if the results came back. She confirms he tested positive for the metagene, leading him to deduce, "Which means Tara..." and she adds that Princess Tara would likely have tested positive as well. Prince Brion concludes, "Which explains why she was taken." A dramatic, suspenseful score then accompanies a title card announcing 'NIGHTWING'S COVERT TEAM INVESTIGATES AFTER META-HUMAN TRAFFICKERS STRIKE IN MARKOVIA,' set against a cosmic background. The narrative cuts to a dramatic moment: a man in a white t-shirt is violently thrown through a door, landing injured with glowing red veins on his arm, indicating a meta-human transformation. He lands in front of two figures: one, identified as Crown Prince Gregor Markov, in a red military uniform holding a sword, and another man in a light blue shirt who gasps "No!" after Gregor, in a solemn voice, announces, "Good evening. Less than 24 hours ago, our own King Viktor and my beloved sister, Queen Ilona, were ruthlessly assassinated." The scene shifts to a dimly lit industrial setting where masked figures in black tactical gear are seen, as a male voiceover explains that "Bedlam is one of the most notorious meta-human trafficking syndicates on the planet," and suspects them behind the assassination of the King and Queen. The final scene shows a nighttime outdoor gathering with string lights, where holographic overlays identify Prince Brion Markov and Crown Prince Gregor Markov holding glasses, as the voiceover continues, "Bedlam may even be connected to the..." before the audio cuts. The sequence hints at political intrigue and meta-human threats in Markovia.

The scene opens at a formal nighttime outdoor gathering, with guests in formal attire mingling under string lights and near a large tent, accompanied by a tense, low background hum. Holographic interfaces identify 'Ecks, Simon' as a geneticist from Markovian Children’s Hospital, and 'Frederick, Delamb' as a Baron and Commander of Security Forces. A calm male voice narrates, identifying Simon X as a world-renowned geneticist who has taken a low-paying job, and referring to Frederick Delamb as "the late Queen's brother" and "Uncle Freddy," who is set to become regent. Prince Brion, in a black tuxedo, appears tense and observant, holding a champagne glass as he converses with a blonde woman in a dark blue strapless gown, who also holds a glass. The woman, speaking with a calm, analytical female voice, questions Brion's "quest to activate your own metagene," asking if it's "really wise." Brion, with a deep, determined, slightly accented male voice, responds that "something daring must be done if I am to save Markovia." The woman then advises him that "the man to speak to is Dr. Simon X." A dramatic title card declares, 'PRINCE BRION TAKES MATTERS INTO HIS OWN HANDS TO AVENGE HIS FAMILY AND HIS COUNTRY.' The narrative then cuts to a dimly lit, industrial interior, where Brion confronts Simon Ecks, who, with a sophisticated, slightly condescending male voice, remarks on a prince's interest in "exploring his metagene potential," but warns the process can be "hit or miss." Brion's expression turns to shock and anger as he shouts, his voice enraged, "You're traffickers! Stealing sick children from a children's hospital!" Simon Ecks calmly retorts, "They're not all sick." Brion, desperate, demands to know, "How many children have you taken? How many have you murdered?" A third man in a green coat with a gold medallion appears, observing the confrontation with a stern gaze, just as the scene erupts into sounds of a loud impact, a struggle, and a grunt of pain, suggesting a violent turn.

event_blocks.summary: In a dimly lit industrial lab, a muscular man, Brion, is trapped inside a transparent cylindrical containment pod, visibly distressed and yelling, "What's going on?", "Stop this!", and "Let me out!" amidst metallic clangs and whooshing sounds, while a woman in a white lab coat with glasses calmly observes him, stating, "Don't worry, my child. It is all for the best." Three authoritative men, including a man in a green coat with a gold medallion, the man in the black tuxedo identified as Ecks, Simon, and a man in a red military uniform identified as Frederick, Baron Delamb, observe the captive. Baron Delamb states, "I only require this organization to operate without me for one night," leading to the realization that "Delamb is an anagram for Bedlam." As Ecks, Simon, is identified as working for Count Vertigo, Brion is released from the pod with a loud electrical discharge, collapsing to the floor in pain, accompanied by grunts and gasps. The woman in the lab coat, now concerned, calls out, "Brion! Brion!" and helps him up, declaring, "I'll show you how," while Brion yells, "You get away from me!" and she pleads, "Brion, please, come on!" A cosmic title card announces: 'DR. JACE QUITS WORKING FOR BEDLAM AND HELPS BRION AND NIGHTWING'S TEAM DEFEAT HIM.' Brion, now free and his body glowing with red veins, runs through a dark, rocky landscape, stepping through flames amidst intense energy sounds and panicked exclamations of "What?" and "What is happening?" He then stands before a group of masked operatives, including the woman in the lab coat and the bald man in the orange and green suit, as a determined male voice declares, "We're shutting this place down."

Man in Green Coat with Gold Medallion.description: A man with short white hair, wearing a green coat with a high collar and a gold medallion around his neck; he appears stern and observant, entering the industrial setting to witness the confrontation.

Fiery Red-Veined Figure.description: A humanoid figure engulfed in red flames with glowing red veins, suggesting a powerful, possibly corrupted or transformed state, identified as Brion, who is heard grunting in pain and shouting, 'You get away from me!', as intense energy sounds accompany his transformation.

Masked Figure in Black.description: A figure in a black hooded outfit with a red emblem on the chest, standing among the team in space, partially obscured, now seen in a dark blue hooded cloak, observing the council from a distance.

Industrial Lab Setting.description: A dimly lit, sterile environment with metal surfaces, glass partitions, and scientific equipment, where metallic clangs, whooshing sounds, electrical discharges, and later intense energy sounds and explosions accompany the events.

Man in Red Military Uniform.description: A man with a beard, wearing a red military-style uniform with gold epaulets and a blue sash, holding a sword; he stands authoritatively and is heard stating in a deep voice, 'I only require this organization to operate without me for one night.' He is identified as Baron Delamb, whose name is revealed to be an anagram for Bedlam.

Man in Black Tuxedo.description: A man with short dark hair and glasses, wearing a black tuxedo with a bow tie, identified as Ecks, Simon; he is referred to as 'X' and linked to Vertigo by another character.

Bald Man in Orange and Green Suit.description: A bald man wearing a form-fitting suit with orange torso and green arms, featuring black accents and a V-shaped emblem on the chest; he appears serious and authoritative, with mechanical attachments on his shoulders, and is heard declaring, 'We're shutting this place down,' as part of the team confronting Bedlam.

Woman in White Lab Coat.description: A woman with brown hair tied in a ponytail, wearing glasses and a white lab coat over a purple top; initially appearing concerned and heard saying in a calm, accented voice, 'Don't worry, my child. It is all for the best,' she later expresses urgent concern for Brion, calling his name, and confidently states, 'I'll show you how,' before pleading, 'Brion, please, come on!'

Holographic Identification Interface.description: A futuristic, glowing interface that overlays character names and titles in blue and yellow boxes, used to identify key individuals at the event and in the facility.

Cosmic Title Card.description: A space-themed background with Earth and a red planet, displaying bold white text: 'DR. JACE QUITS WORKING FOR BEDLAM AND HELPS BRION AND NIGHTWING'S TEAM DEFEAT HIM.'

Man in White T-Shirt.description: A man with brown hair, initially wearing a plain white t-shirt, shown trapped and visibly distressed, yelling 'What's going on?', 'Stop this!', and 'Let me out!' He is identified as Brion, who later, with glowing red veins and engulfed in flames, is heard grunting in pain and shouting, 'You get away from me!', as intense energy sounds accompany his transformation.

Containment Pods.description: Large, transparent, rectangular pods with glowing blue edges, used to hold individuals; the captive is heard struggling and yelling from inside one, followed by sounds of electrical discharge upon his release.

raw_segments: The video opens with a tense close-up of two masked figures in dark tactical gear, as a male voice states, "You've bought the prince." Another voice questions, "So Count Vertigo runs Bedlam," and links the man in the black tuxedo, identified as Ecks, Simon, by adding, "And X works for Vertigo." The scene shifts to a dimly lit industrial lab where a muscular man, later identified as Brion, is trapped inside a transparent cylindrical containment pod, visibly distressed and struggling, yelling, "What's going on?", "Stop this!", and "Let me out!" amidst metallic clangs and whooshing sounds. A woman in a white lab coat with glasses observes him, initially saying in a calm, accented voice, "Don't worry, my child. It is all for the best." Three authoritative men, including a man in a green coat with a gold medallion, the man in the black tuxedo, and a man in a red military uniform, identified as Frederick, Delamb, observe the captive. The man in the red uniform, Baron Delamb, states in a deep, authoritative voice, "I only require this organization to operate without me for one night," prompting another voice to realize, "How did I not notice that Delamb is an anagram for Bedlam?" Brion is then released from the pod with a loud electrical discharge, collapsing to the floor in pain, accompanied by grunts and gasps. The woman in the lab coat, now appearing concerned, calls out, "Brion! Brion!" and helps him up, later declaring with determination, "I'll show you how," while Brion yells, "You get away from me!" and she pleads, "Brion, please, come on!" A title card appears over a cosmic background, stating: 'DR. JACE QUITS WORKING FOR BEDLAM AND HELPS BRION AND NIGHTWING'S TEAM DEFEAT HIM.' Brion, now free, runs through a dark, rocky landscape, his body glowing with red veins as he steps through flames, indicating a transformation or power surge, accompanied by intense energy sounds and panicked exclamations of "What?" and "What is happening?" from unseen figures. He is then seen standing before a group of masked operatives, including the woman in the lab coat and the bald man in the orange and green suit, suggesting a shift in allegiance and the beginning of a confrontation, underscored by a determined male voice stating, "We're shutting this place down."

event_blocks.summary: A dramatic explosion near a rocky cliff at night, accompanied by explosive sounds and a deep growl, transitions to a formal outdoor gathering illuminated by string lights. A massive, fiery, rock-like creature, glowing with molten energy, erupts from the ground, roaring and causing destruction. Amidst shouts of "Brion!" and "Markov!", Markov, identified as Prince Brion, emerges from the flames, confronting the creature. A cry for "Alissimos! Help me!" is heard, followed by an explanation that "Your meta-gene has been activated. Your body is now manipulating geologic forces." The creature collapses amidst sounds of struggle and impact, as Frederick, Delamb, in a red military uniform, stands authoritatively, prompting the exclamation, "Delamb's a meta too!" Frederick, Delamb, stern and commanding, declares, "I know you, uncle. And it's clear your deceptions were the groundwork for a coup against my family and Markovia." When challenged about evidence, he firmly states, "We have enough to mount a lengthy investigation into your affairs," before announcing, "I place you under arrest for the murder of my mother and father." A cosmic title card appears with Earth and a red planet, displaying 'META-HUMANS ARE BANNED FROM MARKOVIA, EVEN PRINCE BRION.' A plea of "Gregor, please, don't do this," is met with the calm, authoritative statement, "You must leave Markovia. Immediately." The title card updates to 'TIGRESS MEETS THE GIRL THAT ALLOWED IN KING AND QUEEN MARKOV'S ASSASSIN.' The scene concludes indoors in a dimly lit hallway where a woman in a beige hooded outfit is confronted by a man in a green coat who grabs her arm and holds a white object, resulting in a sharp impact, a gasp, and a metallic clink as she looks alarmed.

Frederick, Delamb.description: A man with dark hair, wearing a red military-style uniform with gold epaulets and a blue sash, standing authoritatively at the outdoor event, displaying a stern and commanding presence. He speaks with a resolute, authoritative voice, accusing an 'uncle' of a coup against his family and Markovia, and placing him under arrest for the murder of his parents.

Man in Green Coat.description: A man with short white hair, wearing a green coat with a high collar, holding a white object, appearing stern and observant as he confronts and restrains the woman in the beige hooded outfit in a dimly lit hallway, accompanied by sounds of a sharp impact and a metallic clink.

Woman in Beige Hooded Outfit.description: A woman with dark skin, wearing a beige hooded outfit, appearing alarmed and gasping as she is restrained by a man in a green coat in a dimly lit hallway, amidst sounds of a sharp impact and a metallic clink.

Nighttime Outdoor Gathering.description: An outdoor event with string lights, hedges, and a formal atmosphere, featuring a grand building in the background, serving as the initial setting for the confrontation with the lava creature.

Fiery Red-Veined Figure.description: A humanoid figure engulfed in red flames with glowing red veins, suggesting a powerful, possibly corrupted or transformed state, which emerges from the ground with a loud roar and is later defeated amidst sounds of struggle and impact.

Dimly Lit Hallway.description: An indoor corridor with wooden doors and muted lighting, creating a secretive and tense atmosphere for the confrontation between the woman in the beige hooded outfit and the man in the green coat.

Cosmic Title Card.description: A space-themed background with Earth and a red planet, displaying bold white text: 'META-HUMANS ARE BANNED FROM MARKOVIA, EVEN PRINCE BRION' and 'TIGRESS MEETS THE GIRL THAT ALLOWED IN KING AND QUEEN MARKOV'S ASSASSIN,' accompanied by dramatic, swelling music and overlaid with dialogue from other characters.

Markov, Brion (Prince).description: A muscular man with brown hair, initially seen emerging from a fiery explosion, his body covered in glowing red veins and dark rock-like armor, indicating a meta-human transformation; he later appears in a black armored suit, standing defiantly, with his name being shouted amidst the chaos.

The scene quickly shifts to a formal outdoor gathering adorned with string lights, where a massive, fiery, rock-like creature, glowing with molten energy, emerges from the ground, roaring loudly and causing destruction. A voice shouts "Brion!" then "Markov!" as Markov, Brion (Prince), a muscular man in a black armored suit with glowing red veins, emerges from the flames to confront the creature. A voice cries "Alissimos! Help me!" while a female voice explains, "Your meta-gene has been activated. Your body is now manipulating geologic forces." Sounds of struggle and impact accompany the creature's collapse as Frederick, Delamb, a man in a red military uniform, stands authoritatively. A voice exclaims, "Delamb's a meta too!" The scene cuts to a close-up of Frederick, Delamb, who appears stern and commanding, his resolute voice declaring, "I know you, uncle. And it's clear your deceptions were the groundwork for a coup against my family and Markovia." Another male voice retorts, "You have no hard evidence!" to which Frederick, Delamb firmly responds, "We have enough to mount a lengthy investigation into your affairs," before stating, "I place you under arrest for the murder of my mother and father." Dramatic music begins to swell as a cosmic title card appears with Earth and a red planet, displaying the text 'META-HUMANS ARE BANNED FROM MARKOVIA, EVEN PRINCE BRION.' A male voice pleads, "Gregor, please, don't do this," followed by another calm, authoritative male voice stating, "You must leave Markovia. Immediately." The cosmic title card updates with new text: 'TIGRESS MEETS THE GIRL THAT ALLOWED IN KING AND QUEEN MARKOV'S ASSASSIN,' as the dramatic music persists. The final scene shifts indoors to a dimly lit hallway, where a woman in a beige hooded outfit is confronted by a man in a green coat, who grabs her arm and holds a white object. A sharp impact, a gasp, and a metallic clink punctuate this tense, covert moment as she looks alarmed.

event_blocks.summary: The video begins with a close-up of a woman’s eye reflecting a dark, starry sky, accompanied by her pained gasps and cries. In a nighttime graveyard, a man in a green coat and another in a dark jacket dig with shovels, the sounds filling the air, with the man in the green coat stating, "I'll cover them up." A woman in a beige hooded outfit is then seen being dragged from a grave, her face contorted in pain and fear. An eerie, high-pitched electronic sound precedes the appearance of a masked Hooded Archer in a purple-lit forest, observing the scene. Back in the graveyard, the two men are startled by a glowing purple energy beam, prompting the man in the dark jacket to ask, "What was that?" as an intense electronic hum builds. The woman in the beige hooded outfit screams as she crawls out of the grave, covered in dirt, shocking the men. The man in the green coat exclaims, "Holy...", while the man in the dark jacket urges, "Shovel faster!" amidst more struggling sounds. The woman in the beige hooded outfit angrily demands, "What are you doing?!" just before the man in the green coat shouts, "No!" The Hooded Archer, now in a tactical stance, uses a high-tech interface to scan the men, labeling them 'PRINCE BRION' and 'UNKNOWN'. A fiery red energy whip, wielded by one of the men, strikes the Hooded Archer with a distinct sound, causing him to grunt in pain as he falls injured. The woman in the beige hooded outfit, now standing, is engulfed in flames, her face illuminated by the fire, accompanied by intense fiery whooshes and crackling. The man in the dark jacket exclaims, "Whoa," and the man in the green coat asks, "What?" The man in the dark jacket then adds, "Okay, not bad for a dead girl," as the woman, now transformed, powerfully states, "Ain't no kill. No dead. Not dead." Ethereal, melancholic music begins as a cosmic title card displays: 'THE TEAM BRINGS OUR NEW META-TEENS BACK WITH THEM TO GET ANSWERS'. The final scene shows the woman in the beige hooded outfit, identified as "Halo," and a blonde woman in a dark jacket in a sunlit park. The blonde woman explains, "She's lost her memory," and gently asks, "Do you remember anything at all from before we met in the cemetery?"

Man in Green Coat.description: A man with short white hair, wearing a green coat with a high collar, seen digging in the graveyard with a shovel, later reacting in shock to the glowing purple energy beam. He states, 'I'll cover them up,' exclaims 'Holy...', shouts 'No!', and asks 'What?' in confusion. He is scanned and labeled as 'PRINCE BRION'.

Blonde Woman in Dark Jacket.description: A woman with blonde hair, wearing a dark jacket over a black top, seen in a sunlit park, conversing seriously with the woman in the beige hooded outfit. She has a calm, explanatory voice, stating, 'This is Halo. She's lost her memory,' and gently asks, 'Do you remember anything at all from before we met in the cemetery?'

Woman in Beige Hooded Outfit.description: A woman with dark skin, wearing a beige hooded outfit, initially shown being dragged from a grave, then crawling out covered in dirt, and finally standing with a transformed, fiery appearance, suggesting a meta-human awakening. She expresses pained gasps and cries, screams, angrily demands 'What are you doing?!', and later makes a powerful, slightly disjointed statement: 'Ain't no kill. No dead. Not dead.' She is identified as 'Halo' by the Blonde Woman.

Man in Dark Jacket.description: A man with a beard, wearing a dark jacket, digging alongside the man in the green coat, displaying a grim expression and later reacting to the supernatural events. He asks 'What was that?', urgently commands 'Shovel faster!', exclaims 'Whoa,' and makes a sarcastic comment, 'Okay, not bad for a dead girl.' He is scanned and labeled as 'UNKNOWN'.

Fiery Red Energy Whip.description: A glowing, serpentine whip of red energy, wielded by one of the men in the graveyard, used to attack the Hooded Archer, producing a distinct energy whip sound upon impact.

Cosmic Title Card.description: A space-themed background with Earth and Mars, displaying bold white text: 'THE TEAM BRINGS OUR NEW META-TEENS BACK WITH THEM TO GET ANSWERS', serving as a narrative transition.

Sunlit Park Setting.description: A bright, green park with trees and grass, providing a stark contrast to the dark graveyard, where the woman in the beige hooded outfit and the blonde woman in the dark jacket have a serious conversation.

Hooded Archer.description: A muscular man in a green hooded outfit with arrows on his back, initially seen observing from a forest, then using a high-tech interface to scan targets, and later being struck by a fiery energy whip, appearing injured and grunting in pain.

event_blocks.summary: Amidst urgent shouts and a chaotic blur of figures falling through a smoky, blue-lit environment with whooshing, crashing, and explosions, a sustained hum transitions to a dark, blurry interior and a dimly lit corridor. A stone-walled passage opens to a nighttime landscape where a man in a beige vest and a man in a blue jacket with a lantern react with shock to a sudden explosion, preceded by rumbling and mechanical sounds, as distorted growls and frantic shouts are heard with more explosions and metallic clangs. A high-tech interface displays a digital passport for 'Gabrielle Daou' of Qurac, with a male voice revealing her identity as a 16-year-old Karaki refugee who worked as a servant in the Marcovian Royal Palace, prompting an investigation. The scene shifts to a sunlit park where a woman in a beige hooded outfit, identified as 'Brion' and described as a 'walking ball of anger,' glows with fiery energy, her eyes blazing yellow as she channels a powerful energy sphere. Encouraged to continue by a male voice, she is questioned about her abilities, with others expressing curiosity about the 'new color' she has found and its function, while a reassuring voice states they are trying to help. Onlookers, including a man in a blue jacket and a man in a Superman t-shirt, watch with concern as Brion's hands glow intensely yellow, a blonde woman in a dark jacket observes from a distance, and a female voice issues a warning to be careful.

Man in Blue Jacket.description: A man with dark hair, wearing a blue jacket with black and gray accents, standing among a group of onlookers, reacting with concern as the woman in the beige hooded outfit unleashes her power.

Superman T-Shirt Man.description: A man with dark hair wearing a black t-shirt with a red Superman 'S' emblem, standing among a group of onlookers, observing the woman in the beige hooded outfit with a serious expression as she unleashes her power.

Man in Beige Vest.description: A man with a beard, wearing a beige vest over a white shirt, seen in a stone-walled passage, reacting in shock to a sudden, loud explosion.

Stone-Walled Passage.description: A narrow, dimly lit corridor with stone walls, opening to a nighttime landscape with a tank in the distance, suggesting a hidden or military location.

Fiery Energy Sphere.description: A bright, yellow-orange sphere of energy held between the hands of the Woman in Beige Hooded Outfit, indicating her meta-human power manifestation.

Sunlit Park Setting.description: A bright, green park with trees and grass, providing a stark contrast to the dark, tense scenes, where the confrontation between the Woman in Beige Hooded Outfit and the Man in Green Shirt takes place.

Blonde Woman in Dark Jacket.description: A woman with blonde hair, wearing a dark jacket over a black top, standing in the background of the park, watching the confrontation with a concerned and alert expression as the woman in the beige hooded outfit manifests her abilities.

Woman in Beige Hooded Outfit.description: A woman with dark skin, wearing a beige hooded outfit, initially shown in a state of transformation, her eyes glowing yellow and hands channeling a bright energy sphere. She is identified as Gabrielle Daou, a 16-year-old Karaki refugee who worked in the Marcovian Royal Palace, and is referred to as 'Brion,' described by a male voice as 'a walking ball of anger' and 'always on the verge of blowing up,' as she is encouraged to unleash her impressive new powers, with a female voice noting, 'You found a new color,' and an accented female voice asking, 'What does it do?'

Man in Green Shirt.description: A man with brown hair, wearing a green shirt, standing in a sunlit park, confronting the Woman in Beige Hooded Outfit. He is heard encouraging her, saying, 'No, no, keep going. Let's see what you can do,' and 'This is just us, trying to help,' as she manifests her powers, and later suggests, 'Well, um, let's try to find out' what her new ability does.

Holographic Identification Interface.description: A futuristic, glowing interface displaying a digital passport for 'Gabrielle Daou' of Qurac, with a finger pointing at the screen, indicating an identity verification or investigation process, while a male voice reveals her as a 16-year-old Karaki refugee who worked as a servant in the Marcovian Royal Palace.

Man in Blue Jacket with Lantern.description: A man with dark hair, wearing a blue jacket, holding a lantern, standing beside the man in the beige vest, also reacting to the explosion in the stone passage.

Digital Passport Display.description: A glowing, holographic interface showing a passport for Gabrielle Daou, including her photo, nationality, and authorization details, used for identity verification.

raw_segments: The segment opens with a close-up of a woman’s intense brown eyes, accompanied by an urgent female voice exclaiming "Hurry!" and a strained "I...". This quickly transitions into a chaotic, blurred sequence of figures falling through a smoky, blue-lit environment, underscored by intense whooshing, crashing, and a loud explosion. An eerie, sustained hum then accompanies a shift to a dark, blurry interior and a dimly lit corridor with a glowing light. The scene then reveals a stone-walled passage opening to a nighttime landscape with a tank in the distance, where two men—one in a beige vest and another in a blue jacket holding a lantern—react in shock to a sudden, loud explosion, preceded by distant rumbling and mechanical sounds. Distorted growls and frantic shouts are heard amidst more explosions and metallic clangs. The narrative shifts to a high-tech interface displaying a digital passport for 'Gabrielle Daou' of Qurac, as a male voice reveals, "Her real name is Gabrielle Daou, 16 years old, Karaki refugee, was working as a servant in the Marcovian Royal Palace." A finger points at the screen, indicating an investigation. The setting changes to a sunlit park where a woman in a beige hooded outfit, now glowing with fiery energy and her eyes blazing yellow, channels a powerful energy sphere. Dialogue identifies her as "Brion," with a male voice stating, "Brion's a walking ball of anger right now," and another adding, "Always on the verge of blowing up." As she unleashes her power, a male voice encourages, "No, no, keep going. Let's see what you can do," and an accented male voice asks, "Is this a test?" followed by a reassuring, "This is just us, trying to help." A female voice observes, "You found a new color," and an accented female voice asks, "What does it do?" A male voice responds, "Well, um, let's try to find out." The final frames show a group of onlookers, including a man in a blue jacket and a man in a Superman t-shirt, reacting with concern as the woman's hands glow with intense yellow light, while a blonde woman in a dark jacket watches from a distance, all underscored by a female voice warning, "Carefully!"

event_blocks.summary: A car crash sound and a low groan precede a nighttime suburban scene, then shift to a dark outdoor setting where Miss Martian supports a Hooded Figure glowing with purple energy, who groans in distress, prompting Miss Martian to observe, "She's healing herself. It's like a reflex." An eerie, atmospheric music score begins as a Cosmic Title Card appears, stating: 'MISS MARTIAN AND TEAM INVESTIGATE A LEAD WITH THEIR ALLIES ON NEW GENESIS.' The scene transitions to a surreal New Genesis landscape with vibrant purple and blue flora, where a Man in a Fur-Lined Green Outfit, a Robotic Figure, a Man in a Teal and Orange Suit, and a Woman in a Black and Pink Outfit stand together. The Man in the Fur-Lined Green Outfit narrates reports of violence between land-dwelling bugs and New Gods, tales of betrayal, and strange monsters, noting that Mother Box detected no monsters. An Orange-Skinned Warrior bursts through the trees, pointing aggressively, and the Robotic Figure interjects, "Bears likely Frak-Lorian." A towering Skull-Masked Figure states, "But the Orion you met was an impostor." The Man in the Green Outfit projects a hologram of a masked warrior with a High-Tech Device, and the Woman in the Black and Pink Outfit comments on a familiar mind touch, followed by an intense energy sound. The Skull-Masked Figure demands, "Explain yourself, Orion!" The Hooded Figure replies, "We... we can't talk here," and the Woman in the Black and Pink Outfit angrily questions his motives and actions regarding the bugs and New Gods. The Hooded Figure responds to Megan, "I can't talk to you when you're wearing this false form," and the Skull-Masked Figure addresses the Hooded Figure as "little brother," as a deep guttural growl is heard, ending the scene with the menacing Skull-Masked Figure and a Red-Armored Figure standing together amidst another intense energy sound. The scene then shifts to a dark, surreal New Genesis forest with ominous, rumbling sounds and sci-fi whooshes, focusing on the lower body of a towering, armored figure before revealing the Skull-Masked Figure with a white skull-like mask, glowing red eyes, and blue-and-white armor. The Red-Armored Figure, identified as Ma'alefa'ak, wearing a horned skull-like mask with glowing yellow eyes and red armor, is revealed crouched. The Skull-Masked Figure, speaking with a female voice, confronts Ma'alefa'ak, demanding an explanation and calling him a "rabid beast." Ma'alefa'ak rejects the name "Ma'alefa'ak" as a "green name," asserting it's a "symbol" that "scares them" – the "Greens" whom he calls "superstitious and cowardly." He reveals his involvement in a "White Revolution on Mars," having made a deal for help by doing "favors on New Genesis," which the Skull-Masked Figure accuses him of using to sacrifice "the peace of this world for your own." Ma'alefa'ak counters that he's helping the "oppressed" to "rise up and overthrow these so-called new gods." The Skull-Masked Figure turns, revealing the stoic Blue and White Armored Figure behind him. Ma'alefa'ak gestures, and the Red-Armored Figure responds with a defiant posture. A massive, golden, ring-shaped structure descends from the sky, hovering above the forest with an ominous glow. Both Ma'alefa'ak and the Skull-Masked Figure look up with awe and determination, as the Skull-Masked Figure questions his "Psychic manipulation."

Miss Martian.description: A woman with short silver hair, wearing a dark jacket, supporting a hooded figure glowing with purple energy, showing concern and protective posture, her voice heard stating, "She's healing herself. It's like a reflex."

Robotic Figure.description: A tall, humanoid robot with a green glowing chest and eyes, standing beside the man in the green outfit, appearing alert and ready, its robotic voice interjecting, "Bears likely Frak-Lorian."

New Genesis Landscape.description: A surreal alien environment with glowing purple and blue flora, twisted trees, and a dreamlike atmosphere, explicitly named in the dialogue as the setting for the confrontation and the arrival of the descending structure, where a deal has been made.

Man in Fur-Lined Green Outfit.description: A large man with red hair, wearing a fur-lined green outfit and sandals, kneeling and later holding a high-tech device that projects a hologram, his deep male voice narrating, "The Forever People have been investigating reports of violence between the land-dwelling bugs and the New Gods of Supertown," and "Tales of betrayal and strange monsters attacking from on high," adding, "But Mother Box detected no monsters."

Red-Armored Figure.description: A muscular figure in red armor with a horned skull-like mask and glowing yellow eyes, identified by his own male voice as Ma'alefa'ak; he initially crouches in a ready stance, then confronts the Skull-Masked Figure, defiantly rejecting the name 'Ma'alefa'ak' as a 'green name' but embracing it as a 'symbol' to scare the 'Greens,' revealing his involvement in a 'White Revolution on Mars' and a deal on New Genesis to help the 'oppressed' rise up.

Hooded Figure.description: A figure in a dark hooded cloak, glowing with purple energy, appearing injured or in distress, being supported by Miss Martian, heard groaning in pain, later speaking with a male voice, stating "We... we can't talk here" and "I can't talk to you when you're wearing this false form, Megan," and is addressed as "little brother" by the Skull-Masked Figure.

Blue and White Armored Figure.description: A large figure in blue and white armor with a glowing chest, standing stoically behind the Skull-Masked Figure, appearing as part of the hostile group and observing the unfolding events with a silent, imposing presence.

Man in Teal and Orange Suit.description: A man in a teal and orange armored suit with glowing accents, standing with a serious expression, part of the investigative team.

Orange-Skinned Warrior.description: A large, muscular figure with orange skin, white loincloth, and glowing yellow eyes, bursting through trees and pointing aggressively, appearing hostile.

High-Tech Device.description: A rectangular, glowing device held by the man in the green outfit, projecting a hologram of a masked warrior, suggesting advanced alien technology.

Woman in Black and Pink Outfit.description: A woman with long dark hair, wearing a black and pink form-fitting outfit, standing with hands clasped, observing the situation with concern, her voice heard commenting, "There's something familiar about his mind touch," and later angrily questioning, "What are you doing? Why are you even here? And why are you trying to make the bugs hate the New Gods?"

Cosmic Title Card.description: A space-themed background with Earth and Mars, displaying bold white text: 'MISS MARTIAN AND TEAM INVESTIGATE A LEAD WITH THEIR ALLIES ON NEW GENESIS,' serving as a narrative transition.

Skull-Masked Figure.description: A towering figure with a white skull-like mask, glowing red eyes, and blue-and-white armored body, exuding menace and authority; she speaks with a female voice, confronting Ma'alefa'ak, questioning his methods and accusing him of sacrificing New Genesis for his own agenda, and later questioning his 'Psychic manipulation'.

Golden Ring Structure.description: A massive, circular, golden structure with glowing edges, descending from the sky and hovering above the forest, casting an ominous light and suggesting a powerful alien or technological entity arriving on New Genesis, its appearance coinciding with the climax of the verbal confrontation.

raw_segments: The segment opens with a car crash sound and a low groan, revealing a nighttime suburban scene where three men stand in a driveway. The focus quickly shifts to a dark outdoor setting where Miss Martian, a woman with short silver hair, supports a Hooded Figure glowing with purple energy, who groans in distress. Miss Martian's voice is heard saying, "She's healing herself. It's like a reflex." Eerie, atmospheric music begins as a Cosmic Title Card appears over a cosmic backdrop, stating: 'MISS MARTIAN AND TEAM INVESTIGATE A LEAD WITH THEIR ALLIES ON NEW GENESIS.' The scene transitions to a surreal alien landscape on New Genesis, featuring vibrant purple and blue flora. A group of four characters, including a large Man in a Fur-Lined Green Outfit, a Robotic Figure, a Man in a Teal and Orange Suit, and a Woman in a Black and Pink Outfit, stand together. The Man in the Fur-Lined Green Outfit, with a deep male voice, narrates, "The Forever People have been investigating reports of violence between the land-dwelling bugs and the New Gods of Supertown," continuing, "Tales of betrayal and strange monsters attacking from on high," and concluding, "But Mother Box detected no monsters." Suddenly, an Orange-Skinned Warrior bursts through the trees, pointing aggressively. The Robotic Figure, with a distinct robotic voice, interjects, "Bears likely Frak-Lorian." A towering Skull-Masked Figure, with a commanding voice, states, "But the Orion you met was an impostor." The Man in the Green Outfit pulls out a High-Tech Device, projecting a hologram of a masked warrior. The Woman in the Black and Pink Outfit comments, "There's something familiar about his mind touch," followed by an intense energy sound. The Skull-Masked Figure demands, "Explain yourself, Orion!" The Hooded Figure, speaking with a male voice, responds, "We... we can't talk here." The Woman in the Black and Pink Outfit angrily questions, "What are you doing? Why are you even here? And why are you trying to make the bugs hate the New Gods?" The Hooded Figure replies, "I can't talk to you when you're wearing this false form, Megan." The Skull-Masked Figure then addresses the Hooded Figure, "Now, little brother," as a deep guttural growl is heard, and the scene ends with the menacing Skull-Masked Figure and a Red-Armored Figure standing together, accompanied by another intense energy sound.

The video opens in a dark, surreal New Genesis forest, bathed in purple and blue hues, with ominous, rumbling sounds and sci-fi whooshes setting a tense, otherworldly atmosphere. The camera focuses on the lower body of a towering, armored figure before slowly panning up to reveal the Skull-Masked Figure, a menacing entity with a white skull-like mask, glowing red eyes, and blue-and-white armor, exuding authority. As the camera pulls back, the Red-Armored Figure, identified by his own male voice as Ma'alefa'ak, is revealed, wearing a horned skull-like mask with glowing yellow eyes and red armor, initially crouched in a ready stance. The Skull-Masked Figure, speaking with a female voice, confronts Ma'alefa'ak, demanding he "Explain yourself" and calling him a "rabid beast." Ma'alefa'ak defiantly rejects the name "Ma'alefa'ak" as a "green name," asserting it's a "symbol" that "scares them" – referring to the "Greens" whom he calls "superstitious and cowardly." He reveals his involvement in a "White Revolution on Mars," having made a deal to gain help by doing "favors on New Genesis," which the Skull-Masked Figure accuses him of using to sacrifice "the peace of this world for your own." Ma'alefa'ak counters that he's helping the "oppressed" to "rise up and overthrow these so-called new gods." The Skull-Masked Figure then turns, revealing the stoic Blue and White Armored Figure behind him, adding to the sense of a formidable group. Ma'alefa'ak gestures, possibly issuing a command, as the Red-Armored Figure responds with a defiant posture. The tension culminates as a massive, golden, ring-shaped structure descends from the sky, hovering above the forest with an ominous glow. Both Ma'alefa'ak and the Skull-Masked Figure look up, their expressions shifting to a mix of awe and determination, as the Skull-Masked Figure questions his "Psychic manipulation." The atmosphere is thick with foreboding and the sense of an impending conflict in this alien landscape.

event_blocks.summary: In a dark, eerie forest on New Genesis, a towering Skull-Masked Figure in blue and white armor stood menacingly beside a grotesque, multi-eyed creature; as the Skull-Masked Figure gestured with a glowing red-eyed, clawed hand, the environment pulsed with purple and blue hues while a female voice condemned their actions and questioned their justification for controlling kidnapped Earth mediteens, labeling them oppressors. The Skull-Masked Figure, with glowing red eyes, responded in a deep, male voice, asserting that the ends justify the means and inviting the female voice to join him, reminding her of their shared suffering, to which she pleaded to remember their past connection, but he retorted he remembered more. The scene abruptly shifted to a sunlit, rocky canyon where two Red-Armored Figures, each with horned skull masks and red-and-white armor, confronted each other; one lunged, triggering a rockslide with accompanying sounds, followed by a metallic clang as they clashed violently amidst increasingly intense background music. As the Red-Armored Figures continued their fight, the Skull-Masked Figure's deep male voice recalled how the Greens treated them and how the female voice defended them, declaring that a war isn't won on defense. Suddenly, a massive, serpentine creature with dark hair, red skin, and a skull-like face emerged from the ground with a monstrous roar and sounds of impact and destruction, looming over the clashing warriors. As the Serpentine Creature dominated, the Skull-Masked Figure's voice concluded that war is won by giving reign to righteous anger. The scene transitioned to a bright, open sky where a Figure in Dark Blue Hooded Cloak stood surrounded by floating holographic images of various people; the female voice firmly stated "No," just as the Serpentine Creature appeared behind the cloaked figure with its mouth agape, accompanied by a dramatic sound effect. The cloaked figure, facing the looming Serpentine Creature, declared in the female voice, "I've chosen love over anger," as softer, melancholic background music played. The final shot revealed a Group of Heroes standing together in the sky—including a man in a dark blue suit, a woman in a purple top, a man in a black Superman t-shirt, and a white wolf—all facing the approaching threat with determination. The Figure in Dark Blue Hooded Cloak, identified as the female voice, addressed "Macom," stating, "there are better ways to change minds. On Earth, on Mars, and here," as the atmosphere shifted from dark and ominous to bright and heroic, emphasizing the confrontation between the monstrous entities and the assembled team.

Serpentine Creature.description: A massive, monstrous entity with dark, flowing hair, red skin, a skull-like face with glowing yellow eyes, and a long, forked tongue, emerging from the ground with a monstrous roar and sounds of impact/destruction, looming aggressively over the warriors and later behind the cloaked figure.

Group of Heroes.description: A diverse team of heroes standing together in the sky, including a man in a dark blue suit, a woman in a purple top, a man in a black Superman t-shirt, a white wolf, and others, all facing the approaching threat with determination.

Holographic Display.description: A futuristic, glowing green hologram in the background, showing a Y-shaped structure, indicating advanced technology in the setting.

New Genesis Landscape.description: A surreal alien environment with glowing purple and blue flora, twisted trees, and a dreamlike atmosphere, serving as the setting for the confrontation and the arrival of the descending structure.

Figure in Dark Blue Hooded Cloak.description: A figure in a dark blue hooded cloak with a red emblem on the chest, standing among the team in space, partially obscured, now seen in a dark blue hooded cloak, observing the council from a distance, and later in a bright sky surrounded by holographic memories. This figure speaks with a clear, determined female voice, condemning actions as 'wrong,' pleading to 'Remember what we meant to each other,' firmly stating 'No,' declaring 'I've chosen love over anger,' and addressing 'Macom' about 'better ways to change minds' on 'Earth, on Mars, and here'.

Red-Armored Figure.description: A muscular figure in red armor with a horned skull-like mask and glowing yellow eyes, initially crouched in a ready stance, then confronting another Red-Armored Figure face-to-face before turning to look up at the descending structure with defiance, clashing violently with metallic sounds and causing a rockslide.

Skull-Masked Figure.description: A towering figure with a white skull-like mask, glowing red eyes, and blue-and-white armored body, exuding menace and authority; he stands in a twisted forest of New Genesis, gesturing with his hand as if commanding or reacting to the descending structure, and speaks with a deep, commanding male voice, asserting 'The ends justify the means,' recalling 'How the Greens treated us,' and declaring 'It's one by giving reign to our righteous anger'.

Blue and White Armored Figure.description: A large figure in blue and white armor with a glowing chest, standing stoically behind the Skull-Masked Figure, appearing as part of the hostile group and observing the unfolding events with a silent, imposing presence.

raw_segments: The sequence opens in a dark, eerie forest on New Genesis, where a towering Skull-Masked Figure in blue and white armor stands menacingly beside a grotesque, multi-eyed creature. As the Skull-Masked Figure gestures with a clawed hand, his red eyes glowing with malice, and the environment pulses with purple and blue hues, a female voice condemns their actions, stating, "is wrong, no matter how good the intentions. And how do you justify controlling kidnapped Earth mediteens? Now you are the oppressor." The Skull-Masked Figure, with glowing red eyes, responds in a deep, male voice, asserting, "The ends justify the means," and then, "Join me. You know our suffering." The female voice pleads, "Remember what we meant to each other," to which the Skull-Masked Figure retorts, "I remember more than you." The scene shifts abruptly to a sunlit, rocky canyon where two Red-Armored Figures, each with horned skull masks and red-and-white armor, confront each other. One lunges, causing a rockslide with accompanying sounds, followed by a metallic clang as they clash violently, all set against increasingly intense background music. As the Red-Armored Figures continue their violent clash, the deep male voice of the Skull-Masked Figure continues to speak, recalling, "How the Greens treated us. And how you defended us," before declaring, "But a war isn't one on defense." Suddenly, a massive, serpentine creature with dark hair, red skin, and a skull-like face emerges from the ground with a monstrous roar and sounds of impact/destruction, looming over the two clashing warriors. As the Serpentine Creature dominates the scene, the Skull-Masked Figure's voice concludes, "It's one by giving reign to our righteous anger." The scene transitions to a bright, open sky where a figure in a dark blue hooded cloak stands, surrounded by floating holographic images of various people. The female voice firmly states, "No," just as the serpentine creature appears behind the cloaked figure, its mouth agape as if ready to attack, accompanied by a dramatic sound effect. The cloaked figure, facing the looming Serpentine Creature, declares in the female voice, "I've chosen love over anger," as softer, melancholic background music plays. The final shot reveals a group of heroes standing together in the sky—including a man in a dark blue suit, a woman in a purple top, a man in a black Superman t-shirt, and a white wolf—all facing the approaching threat with determination. The female voice, now identified as belonging to the Figure in Dark Blue Hooded Cloak, addresses "Macom," stating, "there are better ways to change minds. On Earth, on Mars, and here," as the atmosphere shifts from dark and ominous to bright and heroic, emphasizing the confrontation between the monstrous entities and the assembled team.

event_blocks.summary: In a surreal, alien forest on New Genesis, bathed in deep purples and blues with twisted, glowing flora, the Skull-Masked Figure, whose glowing red eyes intensify from grim to furious, confronts a group of heroes including the Red-Armored Figure and the Blue and White Armored Figure, accusing a "little bug" of bringing them there and threatening to "crush you like the pest you are" before a whooshing sound and a loud thud. The Skull-Masked Figure roars that "Forager is a traitor! Forager has no place in the Hive!" to which a female voice defends, "This isn't Forager's fault. Please understand, that was never Orion. It was a Martian, feeding your hate." The Skull-Masked Figure's echoing voice threatens, "If Hive sees any of you again... Hive will end you!" The Man in Fur-Lined Green Outfit warns that "The shapeshifter could return in a new form," and notes, "clearly, you cannot return to your Hive." The scene abruptly shifts to a suburban nighttime setting where the Woman in Hijab greets "Brion" and introduces "Forager. He's from New Genesis." The Man in Dark Jacket, with a serious expression, explains, "Forager is in exile from Forager's home." The Man in Fur-Lined Green Outfit's voice states, "From one exile to another. Welcome to Earth," and the Man in Dark Jacket concludes, "Forager thanks, Brion." The final frame displays a text overlay: 'ZATANNA'S POWERS DEVELOP AS SHE CONTINUES TO DEAL WITH HER FATHER'S PAST.'

Man in Dark Jacket.description: A man with a beard, wearing a dark jacket, standing outside a suburban house at night, confronting a woman in a hijab with a serious expression; he speaks with a deep, slightly gravelly male voice, stating "Forager is in exile from Forager's home" and later conveying "Forager thanks, Brion."

Man in Fur-Lined Green Outfit.description: A large man with red hair, wearing a fur-lined green outfit and sandals, kneeling and later holding a high-tech device that projects a hologram, appearing to communicate with the group; he speaks with a calm, authoritative male voice, discussing a "shapeshifter" and the inability to return to the "Hive," and later welcoming "Forager" to Earth as "from one exile to another."

Red-Armored Figure.description: A muscular figure in red armor with a horned skull-like mask and glowing yellow eyes, initially crouched in a ready stance, then confronting the Skull-Masked Figure face-to-face before turning to look up at the descending structure with defiance.

Text Overlay.description: A white text overlay on a black background reading 'ZATANNA'S POWERS DEVELOP AS SHE CONTINUES TO DEAL WITH HER FATHER'S PAST,' indicating a narrative transition to Zatanna's personal storyline.

Skull-Masked Figure.description: A towering figure with a white skull-like mask, glowing red eyes, and a blue-and-white armored body, exuding menace and authority; he gestures aggressively and clenches his fists, his expression shifting from grim to furious, speaking with a deep, menacing, distorted, and echoing male voice, accusing a "little bug" of bringing others here, threatening to "crush you like the pest you are," and declaring "Forager is a traitor" with "no place in the Hive," vowing "Hive will end you."

Woman in Hijab.description: A woman wearing a dark hijab and holding a bundle of red apples, standing outside a house at night, appearing concerned as she is confronted by the Man in Dark Jacket; she speaks with a friendly female voice, introducing "Brion" and "Forager," and clarifying that "Forager" is from New Genesis.

Blue and White Armored Figure.description: A large figure in blue and white armor with a glowing chest, standing stoically behind the Skull-Masked Figure, appearing as part of the hostile group and observing the unfolding events with a silent, imposing presence.

raw_segments: The video opens in a surreal, alien forest on New Genesis, bathed in deep purples and blues with twisted, glowing flora, as a female voice confirms the location. A group of imposing figures, including the Skull-Masked Figure with glowing red eyes and a blue-and-white armored body, confronts a smaller group of heroes. The Skull-Masked Figure, speaking with a deep, menacing voice, accuses a "little bug" of bringing them here, threatening to "crush you like the pest you are." He gestures menacingly, clenches his fists, and his expression shifts from grim to furious, his glowing eyes intensifying, followed by a whooshing sound and a loud thud. The heroes, including the Red-Armored Figure with a horned skull mask and glowing yellow eyes, the Blue and White Armored Figure, and the Man in Fur-Lined Green Outfit, stand in defensive stances, their expressions tense. The Skull-Masked Figure roars, his voice distorted and echoing, declaring "Forager is a traitor! Forager has no place in the Hive!" A female voice defends, stating, "This isn't Forager's fault. Please understand, that was never Orion. It was a Martian, feeding your hate." The Skull-Masked Figure's echoing voice returns, threatening, "If Hive sees any of you again... Any of you... Hive will end you!" The Man in Fur-Lined Green Outfit, speaking with a calm, authoritative male voice, warns that "The shapeshifter could return in a new form," and notes, "clearly, you cannot return to your Hive." The scene then abruptly shifts to a suburban nighttime setting, where a group of civilians, including a man in a dark jacket and a woman in a hijab holding red apples, stand outside a house. The Woman in Hijab, with a friendly female voice, greets "Brion" and introduces, "This is Forager. He's from New Genesis." The Man in Dark Jacket, confronting her with a serious expression, speaks with a deep, slightly gravelly male voice, explaining, "Forager is in exile from Forager's home." The Man in Fur-Lined Green Outfit's voice returns, stating, "From one exile to another. Welcome to Earth." The Man in Dark Jacket's voice concludes, "Forager thanks, Brion." The final frame displays a text overlay: 'ZATANNA'S POWERS DEVELOP AS SHE CONTINUES TO DEAL WITH HER FATHER'S PAST,' indicating a narrative shift to a personal storyline involving Zatanna.

event_blocks.summary: A cosmic title card, 'ZATANNA'S POWERS DEVELOP AS SHE CONTINUES TO DEAL WITH HER FATHER BECOMING DOCTOR FATE,' accompanied by a deep, ethereal hum and shimmering sound effects, introduces the scene. In a sunlit park, Doctor Fate, in golden armor, removes his helmet, revealing his aged face, and asks a blonde woman in a dark jacket, identified as Zatanna's mother, if she understands their agreement. She tearfully confirms, 'Yes. One hour, just like last year and the year before that, and the year before that.' Doctor Fate, her father, states, 'Be grateful Nabu grants you this boon at all,' before tenderly telling Zatanna, who watches with sadness and resolve, 'I love you, Zatanna. And I'll see you next year,' followed by a powerful magical whoosh and building energy sounds. The setting shifts to a dark, industrial warehouse where Zatanna, in a black suit, confronts a demonic figure and a tiger-like creature. Chanting backward, 'Uoy ees I!', Zatanna faces the demonic figure's enraged command, 'Get her, witch!' She summons a glowing yellow ankh, unleashing an energy blast while chanting, 'Reek t'ff, hidioog, yim ying roof, kiyork, stay t'ff, ilog, ankh!' The demonic figure is repelled, clutching a chest wound pulsing with red energy, exclaiming, 'No! There! That doesn't belong to you! Tower of fate! Young, no, no, no! What if Nabu comes back? This is his place of power!' The segment ends with a cosmic title card, 'THE LEAGUE'S COVERT TEAMS CONTINUE TO GROW, EXCITING SOME BUT TROUBLING OTHERS,' and a final magical hum.

Ankh Symbol.description: A glowing yellow ankh, an ancient Egyptian symbol of life, that appears behind Doctor Fate in the park and is later summoned by Zatanna in the warehouse as a source of magical power, radiating intense light.

Blonde Woman in Dark Jacket.description: A woman with blonde hair, wearing a dark jacket, standing beside Doctor Fate in the park, embracing him with a concerned and emotional expression. She speaks with a soft, slightly resigned voice, revealing she is Zatanna's mother and refers to Doctor Fate as 'Daddy'.

Demonic Figure.description: A pale man with black horns, wearing a black suit with red accents, who appears in a dark warehouse. He confronts Zatanna with a sinister expression, shouting commands with a guttural, angry voice, and later expresses pain and concern about Nabu after being struck by Zatanna's energy blast.

Tiger-Like Creature.description: A feline beast with red eyes, sharp teeth, and dark markings, appearing in the warehouse alongside the demonic figure, snarling aggressively before being defeated by Zatanna’s power.

Doctor Fate.description: A man in golden armor with a helmet that splits vertically to reveal his aged face with a white beard and green eyes. He speaks with a deep, resonant, slightly distorted voice when discussing agreements, and a softer, paternal tone when addressing Zatanna, revealing he is her father.

Warehouse Setting.description: A dimly lit industrial space with metal structures and a red-tinged atmosphere, serving as the battleground for Zatanna’s magical confrontation, filled with intense magical energy sounds and battle cries.

Zatanna.description: A woman with dark hair, initially in a blue dress in the park, later in a black suit with a white bow tie in a dark warehouse. She displays intense focus and power, speaking backward spells with a clear, commanding voice, and is revealed to be Doctor Fate's daughter.

Cosmic Title Card.description: A space-themed background with Earth and Mars, displaying bold white text, accompanied by a deep, ethereal hum and shimmering sound effects.

Sunlit Park Setting.description: A bright, green park with trees and grass, providing a peaceful yet emotionally charged backdrop for the dialogue between Doctor Fate and the blonde woman, and Zatanna's silent observation.

raw_segments: The segment opens with a cosmic title card, 'ZATANNA'S POWERS DEVELOP AS SHE CONTINUES TO DEAL WITH HER FATHER BECOMING DOCTOR FATE,' accompanied by a deep, ethereal hum and shimmering sound effects. The scene transitions to a sunlit park where Doctor Fate, in his golden armor, removes his helmet to reveal his aged face, speaking with a deep, resonant voice, asking, "Do you understand the terms of our agreement?" A blonde woman in a dark jacket, embracing him emotionally, responds with a soft, resigned voice, "Yes. One hour, just like last year and the year before that, and the year before that." Doctor Fate, identified as her father, then tells her, "Be grateful Nabu grants you this boon at all." He then tenderly tells Zatanna, who watches with sadness and resolve, "I love you, Zatanna. And I'll see you next year." A powerful magical whoosh and building energy sounds signal a shift to a dark, industrial warehouse. Here, Zatanna, now in a black suit, confronts a demonic figure and a tiger-like creature. She chants a backward spell, "Uoy ees I!" (I see you!), as the demonic figure, with a guttural, angry voice, shouts, "Get her, witch!" Zatanna raises her hands, summoning a glowing yellow ankh, and unleashes a powerful energy blast while chanting a complex backward spell, "Reek t'ff, hidioog, yim ying roof, kiyork, stay t'ff, ilog, ankh!" The demonic figure is thrown back, clutching his chest as red energy pulses from his wound, exclaiming in pain, "No! There! That doesn't belong to you!" He continues, "Tower of fate! Young, no, no, no! What if Nabu comes back? This is his place of power!" The segment concludes with another cosmic title card, 'THE LEAGUE'S COVERT TEAMS CONTINUE TO GROW, EXCITING SOME BUT TROUBLING OTHERS,' accompanied by a final magical hum.

event_blocks.summary: A cosmic title card, 'THE LEAGUE'S COVERT TEAMS CONTINUE TO GROW, EXCITING SOME BUT TROUBLING OTHERS,' sets a tense tone, followed by characters choosing cryptonyms: "Voyager is... Voyager," "I'm Halo. It was the first name you gave me and I like it. I'm Violet at home, I'm Halo during a mission. Halo," and "Doctor Jace had a name for the powers in my bloodline. She called it the Geo-Force." Garth, in dark blue armor with glowing blue lines, stands solemnly with a concerned group including a woman in a black hooded outfit, a man in a tactical suit, and Markov, the rocky-skinned Geo-Force, under a starry sky. A remark about leading another team is met with exasperation. In a futuristic, purple-lit council chamber, Nightwing, a man in a Superman t-shirt, and a large armored figure join others around a glowing table, where a strong female voice asserts, "Between the seven of us, we're coordinating six teams of heroes, none of whom know we're working together." She confronts "Diana" about fighting fire with fire, like the Light, questioning if they represent darkness. A hesitant voice suggests a poor choice of words, but the strong female voice insists she dislikes keeping League members in the dark. Batman, arms crossed in a dimly lit cave, is confronted by a holographic Woman in Purple and Blue Armored Suit, with Robin observing, as Batman argues for deniability, questioning if Superman would lie under oath. The hologram challenges him. Later, in a high-tech command center, Batman, Nightwing, Miss Martian, Robin, and a young woman in a wheelchair discuss a "Reach device" found after busting a meta-trafficking ring. The tech expert explains it's a "metahuman fail-safe" and is en route to Dr. Stone at Star Labs Detroit, where it caused an accident affecting his son, Victor Stone. Victor argues with his father, the Man in White Lab Coat, about his ignored life, but an electrical surge from a cable startles Victor, leading to a chaotic explosion. Victor is found injured and unconscious amidst wreckage, with the glowing red Reach device nearby. Dr. Stone, using a "Mother Box" alien healing device despite warnings, places it on Victor's chest, triggering a violent red energy surge, crackling, and explosions. Victor's body erupts with pulsating red energy, revealing a mechanical, armored structure beneath his skin, culminating in a full-body suit of dark, angular armor with glowing red lines and a central red eye, his transformation complete. A close-up of a red, cracked surface with a glowing red eye and guttural groans precedes Victor, partially cybernetic, asking, "What have you done to me?!" His voice distorts as he declares, "You ruined me! Murdered my future! Now... I'll murder you!" with a glowing red hand. A title card, 'VILOET DISCOVERS NEW POWERS LINKED TO VICTOR'S NEW POWERS,' appears. Violet Harper opens a boom tube, described as being like a Mother Box. A young man in a hockey jersey and a woman in a hijab are enveloped in purple energy. Violet, now with glowing purple eyes, calmly states, "I didn't know it was doable until I did it. Then it seemed, well, natural," before asking, "Where am I?" as a swirling vortex forms and she projects an energy blast. Victor and others react to the power surge engulfing the lab, hinting at a connection between Victor’s cybernetic evolution and the emergence of new powers in others, ending with a panicked, cut-off exclamation, "Any of them...".

Nightwing.description: A young man in a black tactical suit with blue accents and a mask, displaying intense focus and using a high-tech gauntlet to scan a device in a warehouse; his blue eyes are sharp and determined.
