Woman in Purple and Blue Armored Suit.description: A woman with dark hair, wearing a purple and blue armored suit with a star emblem on the chest, glowing with a blue aura, and holding a sword; she exudes confidence and readiness, appearing as a hologram, and speaks with a strong, clear, confident voice, questioning the team's covert operations and their 'anti-Light' stance, and is addressed as 'Diana' by another male voice.

Garth.description: A muscular man with dark hair tied back, wearing a dark blue armored suit with glowing light-blue lines; he stands solemnly at the United Nations Meta-Human Summit, identified as Ambassador of Atlantis.

Nighttime Outdoor Gathering.description: An outdoor event with string lights, hedges, and a formal atmosphere, featuring a grand building in the background, serving as the initial setting for the confrontation with the lava creature.

Robin.description: A young man in a red and black suit with a yellow 'R' emblem on the chest, standing beside Batman, observing the confrontation with a serious expression.

Batman.description: A figure in a black armored suit with a bat emblem, standing with arms crossed, displaying a stern and commanding presence. He is heard saying, 'If it comes to that, as a princess of Themyscira, I expect you to use your diplomatic status to avoid taking the stand at all.'

Futuristic Council Chamber.description: A high-tech, purple-lit room with glowing tables and advanced technology, where heroes gather for strategic discussions, emphasizing the League's covert operations.

Cosmic Title Card.description: A space-themed background with Earth and Mars, displaying bold white text: 'THE LEAGUE'S COVERT TEAMS CONTINUE TO GROW, EXCITING SOME BUT TROUBLING OTHERS.'

Markov, Brion (Prince).description: A muscular man with brown hair, initially seen emerging from a fiery explosion, his body covered in glowing red veins and dark rock-like armor, indicating a meta-human transformation; he later appears in a black armored suit, standing defiantly, and is identified by a male voice as having powers called the 'Geo-Force' in his bloodline.

Miss Martian.description: A woman with dark hair, wearing a purple and blue armored suit with a star emblem on the chest, glowing with a blue aura, and holding a sword; she is present in the command center and is heard asking, 'What does this Reach device do?' and 'And where's the Reach device currently?'

Reach Device.description: A small, rectangular device with glowing red and blue lights, identified by Dr. Stone as a "Mother Box" used by aliens to heal. It causes an electrical surge and subsequent explosion, leading to Victor Stone's injury and transformation.

Lab Setting.description: A dimly lit, sterile laboratory with glass partitions and scientific equipment, where Victor’s transformation occurs amidst mechanical whirring, energy crackling, and metallic clangs, and where the subsequent energy surge unfolds with intense blasts and impacts, creating a tense and apocalyptic atmosphere.

Victor Stone.description: A muscular young man with short dark hair, now partially cybernetic with red energy lines coursing through his body, a central red eye on his chest, and a mechanical arm; he transitions from pain to determination, emitting deep, guttural groans and roars, then speaking with a strained, pained voice asking "What have you done to me?!", before his voice deepens and distorts into a menacing tone, declaring "You ruined me! Murdered my future! Now... I'll murder you!", indicating evolving and hostile powers.

Man in White Lab Coat.description: A man with a beard and glasses, wearing a white lab coat, who appears concerned and urgent, speaking with a worried tone, asking "Vic! Vic! Can you hear me? Are you all right?" as he observes Victor’s transformation, and later a panicked "Any of them..." as the energy surge unfolds.

Woman in White Lab Coat.description: A woman with brown hair tied in a ponytail, wearing a white lab coat over a purple top, standing in the background. She expresses urgent pleas ("Silas, please!"), questions ("What is that?"), and warnings ("Dr. Stone, you can't use that thing on Victor!"), later reacting with a shocked gasp ("What?") and a horrified whisper ("No...") as the transformation unfolds.

Viloet.description: A woman with dark skin wearing a dark hijab, identified by a narrator as "Violet Harper," who is shown undergoing a transformation with glowing purple eyes and hands, projecting a powerful energy blast; she speaks with a calm, reflective voice explaining her new abilities, "I didn't know it was doable until I did it. Then it seemed, well, natural," before expressing confusion with "Where am I?".

Woman in Hijab.description: A woman with dark skin wearing a dark hijab, initially shown in a state of transformation with glowing purple eyes and hands, projecting a powerful energy blast, indicating she has developed new powers linked to Victor’s evolution.

raw_segments: The segment opens with a cosmic title card, 'THE LEAGUE'S COVERT TEAMS CONTINUE TO GROW, EXCITING SOME BUT TROUBLING OTHERS,' set against Earth and Mars, accompanied by tense, building background music. A deep, calm male voice states, "Tonight, you choose your cryptonym." This is followed by a hesitant female voice saying, "Voyager is... Voyager," then an energetic female voice confidently declaring, "I'm Halo. It was the first name you gave me and I like it. I'm Violet at home, I'm Halo during a mission. Halo." A calm, slightly gravelly male voice then explains, "Doctor Jace had a name for the powers in my bloodline. She called it the Geo-Force," as the scene transitions to a nighttime outdoor setting where Garth, a muscular man in a dark blue armored suit with glowing light-blue lines, stands solemnly. He is joined by a group of three heroes, including a woman in a black hooded outfit with purple accents (potentially Halo), a man in a dark green and black tactical suit, and a large, rocky-skinned figure with glowing red eyes (Markov, Brion, likely the Geo-Force), all appearing concerned and alert under a starry sky. Another male voice remarks, "And just like that, you're leading another team," to which a younger, slightly exasperated male voice responds, "Ah, please don't rub it in." The scene shifts to a futuristic, purple-lit council chamber where diverse heroes, including Nightwing in a black suit with a blue bat emblem, a man in a Superman t-shirt, and a large armored figure, stand around a glowing table. A strong, clear female voice asserts, "Between the seven of us, we're coordinating six teams of heroes, none of whom know we're working together." A deep, serious male voice then addresses "Diana," stating, "It's what we all agreed to, Diana. Taking a page from the Light's own playbook, to fight fire with fire." A slightly lighter male voice adds, "We're kind of the anti-Light," prompting the strong female voice to question, "And that's a good thing? The opposite of Light is darkness. Is that what we represent?" A hesitant male voice suggests, "Um, maybe that was a poor choice of words," but the strong female voice retorts, "Or maybe it wasn't. I don't like keeping the other members of the League in the dark." The scene cuts to a dimly lit, high-tech cave where Batman stands with arms crossed, confronted by a glowing blue holographic Woman in Purple and Blue Armored Suit (likely Diana/Wonder Woman), with Robin observing. The deep, serious male voice (likely Batman) continues, "Worst case scenario, they need real deniability. Do you expect Superman to lie under oath?" The strong female voice (the hologram) challenges back, "Do you expect me..." as the minute concludes.

The video opens in a high-tech command center bathed in blue light, where Batman, Nightwing, Miss Martian, Robin, and a young woman in a wheelchair are gathered. A male voice, identified as Batman, is heard saying, "If it comes to that, as a princess of Themyscira, I expect you to use your diplomatic status to avoid taking the stand at all." The tense atmosphere is underscored by ominous, low-frequency background music. A title card reveals the team uncovered a 'Reach device' after busting a meta-trafficking ring. Miss Martian then asks, "What does this Reach device do?" The young woman in the wheelchair, a tech expert, explains that Blue Beetle had previously translated the Reach text on the machine, stating it's a "metahuman fail-safe." Miss Martian further inquires, "And where's the Reach device currently?" to which the young woman replies, "On route back to Dr. Stone at Star Labs Detroit." A close-up emphasizes Miss Martian and the young woman's concern. Another title card states the Reach device caused an accident in Dr. Stone’s lab, affecting his son, Victor Stone. The scene shifts to a lab setting, accompanied by mechanical whirring. Victor Stone, a muscular young man in a sleeveless hoodie, is heard shouting, "Dad! You missed me crushing it at my game, and the celebration after. This is my life you're ignoring! My actual life!" to the Man in White Lab Coat, who calmly responds, "Which we can discuss when you've calmed yourself down, boy." Victor, agitated, retorts, "Boy? Now you're boying me?" Suddenly, a cable emits a loud electrical surge and high-pitched whine, startling Victor as the lab lights flicker. The scene escalates into a chaotic explosion with a massive boom and falling debris. Victor Stone is shown lying injured, bloodied, and unconscious on the floor. The Woman in White Lab Coat exclaims, "I found him!" The Man in White Lab Coat gasps, "No!" and then, amidst distressed grunts and heavy breathing, urgently says, "Hold on, son. I've got you. Got you," as he stands amidst the wreckage with the Woman in White Lab Coat, one of them holding the glowing red Reach device, the apparent source of the accident.

In a dimly lit, sterile lab, three scientists in white coats gather around a severely injured young man, Victor Stone, lying bloody on the floor. The Man in White Lab Coat, identified as Dr. Stone, kneels beside Victor, holding a small, glowing red device. As a woman's urgent voice pleads, "Silas, please! What is that?", Dr. Stone, with a determined "Got it!", explains it's a "Mother Box" used by aliens to heal. Despite the woman's warning, "Dr. Stone, you can't use that thing on Victor!", he defiantly asserts his expertise, declaring, "When I say it will work, it will work! It has to." He places the device on Victor's chest, triggering a violent surge of red energy accompanied by intense electrical crackling, whirring, and explosions, shattering glass. Victor's body erupts with a pulsating, spider-like red energy, his face contorting in agony as deep, guttural groans and roars escape him amidst mechanical grinding and metallic groans. The red light intensifies, revealing a mechanical, armored structure beneath his skin, culminating in a full-body suit of dark, angular armor with glowing red lines and a central red eye. The Woman in White Lab Coat gasps "What?" and then whispers a horrified "No...", as the newly transformed cybernetic figure rises, radiating an ominous red glow, his transformation complete and irreversible, shifting the lab's atmosphere from clinical to apocalyptic.

The sequence opens with a close-up of a red, cracked surface, revealing a glowing red eye within a mechanical structure, accompanied by deep, guttural groans and intense mechanical whirring, suggesting a painful transformation. The camera pulls back to show Victor Stone, now partially cybernetic with red energy lines and a central red eye on his chest, standing in a dimly lit lab. A man in a white lab coat urgently calls out, "Vic! Vic! Can you hear me? Are you all right?" Victor, his voice strained and pained, responds, "What? Oh... Oh... What have you done to me?!" His expression shifts from pain to determination as his voice deepens and distorts, declaring, "You ruined me! Murdered my future! Now... I'll murder you!" while raising a hand glowing with red energy that transitions to purple, indicating a shift in power amidst building energy sounds. A title card appears: 'VILOET DISCOVERS NEW POWERS LINKED TO VICTOR'S NEW POWERS,' set against a cosmic backdrop. A narrator's voice explains, "Violet Harper opened a boom tube, as if Violet Harper was a Mother Box." The scene cuts to a young man in a hockey jersey, then a woman in a hijab, both enveloped in purple energy, suggesting similar transformations. The woman, identified as Violet Harper, calmly states, "I didn't know it was doable until I did it. Then it seemed, well, natural," before asking, "Where am I?" The energy intensifies, forming a swirling vortex, and she is shown with glowing purple eyes, projecting a powerful energy blast accompanied by intense blasts and explosions. The lab setting is shown again, with Victor and the others reacting to the surge of power as the purple energy engulfs the room, hinting at a connection between Victor’s cybernetic evolution and the emergence of new powers in others, ending with the lab coat man's panicked, cut-off exclamation, "Any of them...".

event_blocks.summary: In a dimly lit lab filled with distorted electronic sounds and energy surges, Victor Stone, exhibiting cybernetic enhancements and distress, groans as Viloet observes his transformation, calmly stating, "Wait for it," before a violent purple energy surge erupts from him, shaking the lab and causing the Man in White Lab Coat to recoil. Viloet then declares, "I think I've cleansed you," but Victor, distressed, attempts to leave, insisting, "I need to leave before I do something that we both..." The Man in White Lab Coat interrupts, attributing Victor's words to the "Father Box talking," but Victor reiterates his desire to leave, "I don't want to be anywhere near you right now. Let's go." Despite the Man in White Lab Coat's plea, "Victor, wait!" Victor and Viloet are pulled into a swirling purple vortex, disappearing with a whoosh and explosion. Subsequently, outside a house, Superman T-Shirt Man, Nightwing, and a woman with glasses discuss a "dissected Mother Box" at Bedlam Central, with Nightwing explaining that the "burst of energy" was likely the Mother Box's soul escaping, as "Mother Boxes are living computers." In a sunlit park, Doctor Fate embraces the Blonde Woman in Dark Jacket while Zatanna, in a black suit with a white bow tie, remarks, "Hello, Megan! Doctor Fate sensed an old soul in Halo's very young body." Later, in a graveyard at night, the Man in Green Coat is digging when the Figure in Dark Blue Hooded Cloak approaches and reacts with shock to a purple energy beam. The narrative concludes in a dimly lit industrial setting where the Woman in Hijab and other heroes stand with serious expressions, facing an unseen threat.

Nightwing.description: A young man in a black tactical suit with blue accents and a mask, displaying intense focus and using a high-tech gauntlet to scan a device in a warehouse; his blue eyes are sharp and determined. His clear, analytical male voice discusses a dissected Mother Box, stating, 'When we were at Bedlam Central, I spotted a dissected Mother Box,' and later clarifies, 'Not just dead. Murdered. Mother Boxes are living computers. That burst of energy? It was probably Mother Box's soul escaping.'

Man in Green Coat.description: A man with short white hair, wearing a green coat with a high collar, seen digging in the graveyard with a shovel, later reacting in shock to the glowing purple energy beam.

Blonde Woman in Dark Jacket.description: A woman with blonde hair, wearing a dark jacket over a black top, standing beside Doctor Fate in the park, embracing him with a concerned and emotional expression.

Victor Stone.description: A muscular young man with short dark hair, now partially cybernetic with red energy lines coursing through his body, a central red eye on his chest, and a mechanical arm; he transitions from pain to determination, then to a purple energy state, indicating evolving powers. His deep male voice conveys distress and urgency as he groans in pain and states, 'I need to leave before I do something that we both...' and 'I don't want to be anywhere near you right now. Let's go.'

Man in White Lab Coat.description: A man with a beard and glasses, wearing a white lab coat, who appears concerned and urgent as he observes Victor’s transformation and the subsequent energy surge in the lab. His urgent male voice asks, 'What did you do to him?' asserts, 'That's the Father Box talking, not you, son!' and cries, 'Victor, wait!'

Doctor Fate.description: A man in golden armor with a helmet that splits vertically to reveal his aged face, featuring a white beard and green eyes; he is shown embracing a woman in a dark jacket in a sunlit park, then later in a warehouse, his presence radiating mystical authority.

Figure in Dark Blue Hooded Cloak.description: A figure in a dark blue hooded cloak with a red emblem on the chest, standing among the team in space, partially obscured, now seen in a dark blue hooded cloak, observing the council from a distance.

Superman T-Shirt Man.description: A man with dark hair wearing a black t-shirt with a red Superman 'S' emblem, standing among a group of onlookers, observing the woman in the beige hooded outfit with a serious expression.

Viloet.description: A woman with dark skin wearing a dark hijab, shown undergoing a transformation with glowing purple eyes and projecting energy, identified as discovering new powers linked to Victor Stone’s cybernetic evolution. Her calm, slightly ominous female voice says, 'Wait for it,' and 'I think I've cleansed you.'

Zatanna.description: A woman with dark hair, initially in a blue dress in a sunlit park, later in a black suit with a white bow tie in a dark warehouse, displaying intense focus and power as she summons a glowing ankh and unleashes energy against a demonic foe. Her clear, informative female voice adds details about the Mother Box, saying, 'There was a burst of energy, then the machine went dead,' and later exclaims, 'Hello, Megan! Doctor Fate sensed an old soul in Halo's very young body. And the first time I saw Halo...'

Woman in Hijab.description: A woman with dark skin wearing a dark hijab, initially shown in a state of transformation with glowing purple eyes and hands, projecting a powerful energy blast, indicating she has developed new powers linked to Victor’s evolution.

raw_segments: The sequence opens in a dimly lit lab with a tense atmosphere, where Victor Stone, now partially cybernetic with red energy lines and a glowing red eye on his chest, groans in visible pain amidst intense, distorted electronic sounds and energy surges. A woman in a dark hijab, Viloet, stands nearby, her expression shifting from concern to shock as she witnesses his transformation. The Man in White Lab Coat urgently asks, "What did you do to him?" Viloet calmly responds, "Wait for it," as Victor's body erupts in a violent purple energy surge, causing the lab to shake and the Man in White Lab Coat to recoil. Viloet then states, "I think I've cleansed you." Victor, his voice distressed, declares, "I need to leave before I do something that we both..." but is interrupted by the Man in White Lab Coat, who asserts, "That's the Father Box talking, not you, son!" Victor insists, "I don't want to be anywhere near you right now. Let's go." As Victor attempts to leave, the Man in White Lab Coat cries, "Victor, wait!" but it's too late; the energy forms a swirling vortex, and Victor and Viloet are pulled into the purple portal, disappearing with a loud whoosh and explosion. The scene cuts to a different location, where a group of heroes, including Superman T-Shirt Man, Nightwing, and a woman with glasses, stand outside a house, looking concerned. A male voice, likely Nightwing, explains, "When we were at Bedlam Central, I spotted a dissected Mother Box." A female voice, likely Zatanna, adds, "There was a burst of energy, then the machine went dead." Nightwing clarifies, "Not just dead. Murdered. Mother Boxes are living computers. That burst of energy? It was probably Mother Box's soul escaping." The focus shifts to a sunlit park where Doctor Fate, in his golden armor, embraces a blonde woman in a dark jacket, the Blonde Woman in Dark Jacket, as Zatanna, in a black suit with a white bow tie, stands nearby. Zatanna's voice is heard again, exclaiming, "Hello, Megan! Doctor Fate sensed an old soul in Halo's very young body. And the first time I saw Halo..." The scene then transitions to a graveyard at night, where the Man in Green Coat is digging with a shovel, and a figure in a dark blue hooded cloak, the Figure in Dark Blue Hooded Cloak, approaches, reacting in shock to a sudden purple energy beam. The final shot shows a group of heroes, including the Woman in Hijab, standing together in a dimly lit industrial setting, their expressions serious as they face an unseen threat.

event_blocks.summary: In a smoky graveyard, a figure in a dark hooded outfit falls amidst green mist and debris as Violet explains Vertigo's crew buried a presumed metahuman, while a Woman in a Beige Hooded Outfit, eyes glowing white, reacts in distress to Violet's speculation that Gabrielle Dowd, who interjects "That's me!", died and was resurrected by a Mother Box's soul energy. The Superman T-Shirt Man questions Violet's healing abilities, to which Victor Stone's voice begins to respond about being healed, but Gabrielle Dowd clarifies she only adjusted his internal technology, leading the Superman T-Shirt Man to declare her a living Mother Box and another male voice to note a Halo cleanse is temporary. The scene shifts to a suburban house at dusk where a Woman in a Dark Hijab confronts a Man in a Dark Jacket, and inside, a Man in a White Hoodie with a mechanical arm screams in agony, purple energy crackling across his injured body, underscored by intense, building electronic music and Victor's scream. A text overlay reads 'THE TEAM FINDS OTHER-WORLDLY SOLUTIONS TO HEAL VICTOR.' In a lab, Violet, the Woman in a Black and Pink Outfit, and the Superman T-Shirt Man observe a Woman in a White Lab Coat as Victor Stone, glowing with purple energy and red lines, is in a containment pod, with Violet requesting a Mother Box diagnostic and explaining that placing Victor in the Mobius Chair of Metron, the inventor of Mother Boxes, Father Boxes, and Boom Tubes, is his only hope. The final shot shows Victor Stone in the Mobius Chair, his body transformed with glowing blue and red energy, his face contorted, as the team watches in shock, with Violet concluding the chair has the only powerful enough connection.

Man in White Hoodie.description: A muscular man in a white hoodie with a mechanical arm, shown in pain with purple energy crackling across his body, indicating a cybernetic transformation. A male voice, likely his, notes that 'a Halo cleanse is only a temporary solution.'

Woman in Beige Hooded Outfit.description: A woman with dark skin wearing a beige hooded outfit, shown in a state of distress with glowing white eyes, suggesting a supernatural or meta-human transformation. A young female voice, identified as Gabrielle Dowd, interjects 'That's me!' when her resurrection is discussed, and later clarifies her actions regarding Victor's technology, stating, 'Actually, I don't believe I did heal you, Victor. I think I only made adjustments to your internal technology.'

Man in Dark Jacket.description: A man with a beard, wearing a dark jacket, standing outside a suburban house at night, confronting the woman in the hijab with a stern expression.

Victor Stone.description: A muscular young man with short dark hair, now partially cybernetic with red energy lines coursing through his body, a central red eye on his chest, and a mechanical arm; he is shown in a containment pod and later seated in a high-tech chair, glowing with purple and blue energy, indicating evolving powers. He is heard screaming in agony as purple energy crackles around him, and earlier states, 'Well, she healed me, that's why I...'

Lab Setting.description: A dimly lit, sterile laboratory with glass partitions and scientific equipment, where Victor’s transformation occurs and the energy surge unfolds, creating a tense and apocalyptic atmosphere.

Superman T-Shirt Man.description: A man with dark hair wearing a black t-shirt with a red Superman 'S' emblem, standing among a group of onlookers, observing the transformation with a serious expression. He is heard speaking to Violet, stating 'But Violet, you've never been able to heal anyone but yourself,' and later adds, 'More proof she's a living Mother Box.'

Woman in White Lab Coat.description: A woman with brown hair tied in a ponytail, wearing a white lab coat over a purple top, standing in the background with a shocked expression as the transformation unfolds.

Text Overlay.description: A white text overlay on a black background reading 'THE TEAM FINDS OTHER-WORLDLY SOLUTIONS TO HEAL VICTOR,' indicating a narrative focus on Victor Stone's healing and transformation.

Woman in Black and Pink Outfit.description: A woman with long dark hair, wearing a black and pink form-fitting outfit with a red headband, holding a glowing red book, standing beside the Superman T-Shirt Man in a lab setting. She is identified as Violet and narrates the initial events, speculating about Gabrielle Dowd's resurrection, and later explains the Mother Box's diagnostic and the significance of Metron's Mobius Chair, concluding, 'His Mobius Chair is the only thing with a powerful enough connection...'

Woman in Hijab.description: A woman with dark skin wearing a dark hijab, standing outside a suburban house at dusk, confronting a man with a serious and concerned expression.

raw_segments: The segment opens with a chaotic, smoky graveyard scene where a figure in a dark hooded outfit falls amidst green mist and debris, as a female voice, identified as Violet, explains that "Vertigo's crew" was burying someone they believed to be a powerful metahuman. The scene shifts to a close-up of a Woman in a Beige Hooded Outfit, her eyes glowing white with shock, looking upward in distress, as Violet speculates that "Gabrielle Dowd did die," but was resurrected by "the Mother Box's soul energy." A young female voice, Gabrielle Dowd, interjects, "That's me!" A male voice, the Superman T-Shirt Man, then addresses Violet, stating, "But Violet, you've never been able to heal anyone but yourself." Victor Stone's voice interjects, "Well, she healed me, that's why I..." before Gabrielle Dowd clarifies, "Actually, I don't believe I did heal you, Victor. I think I only made adjustments to your internal technology." The Superman T-Shirt Man adds, "More proof she's a living Mother Box," and another male voice notes that "a Halo cleanse is only a temporary solution." The scene transitions to a suburban house at dusk, where a Woman in a Dark Hijab confronts a Man in a Dark Jacket. Inside, a group of heroes gathers, including a Man in a White Hoodie with a mechanical arm, who is visibly injured with purple energy crackling across his body, screaming in agony. Intense, building electronic music underscores Victor's agonizing scream and crackling energy. A text overlay appears: 'THE TEAM FINDS OTHER-WORLDLY SOLUTIONS TO HEAL VICTOR.' The scene cuts to a lab where Violet, the Woman in a Black and Pink Outfit, holds a glowing red book, standing beside the Superman T-Shirt Man. They observe a Woman in a White Lab Coat as Victor Stone is shown in a containment pod, his body glowing with purple energy and red lines. Violet's voice explains, "Mother Box, run a diagnostic. She believes your only hope is to place you in the Mobius Chair of Metron," continuing to detail "The New God Metron," who "invented Mother Boxes, Father Boxes, and Boom Tubes among other things." The final shot reveals Victor Stone seated in a high-tech chair, his body fully transformed with glowing blue and red energy, his face contorted in pain or power, as the team watches in shock, with Violet concluding, "His Mobius Chair is the only thing with a powerful enough connection..."

event_blocks.summary: Victor Stone undergoes a painful cybernetic transformation, his body wracked by purple and blue energy as a scientific female voice attempts to purge a Fatherbox's influence while an analytical male voice deems the process worthy of study, prompting Victor's father, a Man in a White Lab Coat, to desperately plead for his son's life. The surge culminates in a massive blue explosion, leaving Victor with a glowing red eye and a cybernetic chest device, stabilized by the analytical male voice. Concurrently, a Justice League/S.T.A.R. Labs press conference announces the opening of a metahuman youth center, coinciding with the discovery of a meta-trafficking ring player potentially linked to Brion's sister. The investigation reveals 'goggle goggles,' VR devices from Gretchen Goode's Good World Studios, used to test for the metagene, brainwash metahumans, and send them to 'depot' locations, with one route highlighted on a digital map from Beverly Hills to Encino. The Justice League and team plan simultaneous depot takedowns. In a futuristic council chamber, heroes discuss the 'Tara connection' to Greater Bialya, leading to the revelation that 'Granny' is Gretchen Goode, also known as 'Granny Goodness,' whose shell company purchased an abandoned mall in Greater Bialya, identified as a meta-depot. A dark web list reveals 'item 16: female, age 15, Earth powers,' identified as Tara, and the depot is revealed to be a fight club for meta-teen gladiators. In a futuristic chamber, a man in a purple and black armored suit is auctioned off, with the starting bid for Tara announced at 500,000 US dollars. Subsequently, the armored man embraces a man in a tuxedo, declaring his sister free and safe. A title card announces the League and Team uncovering Granny Goodness's secrets, and Granny Goodness, with a sinister smile, states a plan is in place.

Victor Stone.description: A muscular young man with short dark hair, now fully cybernetic with red energy lines coursing through his body, a central red eye on his forehead, and a mechanical arm; he is shown in a high-tech chair, convulsing as blue and purple energy surges through him, culminating in a transformation that grants him a glowing red eye and chest device.

Man in White Lab Coat.description: A man with a beard and glasses, wearing a white lab coat, who appears concerned and urgent as he observes Victor’s transformation. He speaks with an emotional, urgent tone, identifying Victor as 'my son' and 'Vic,' and desperately calls for the process to stop and for 'Motherbox' to save him.

Woman in White Lab Coat.description: A woman with brown hair tied in a ponytail, wearing a white lab coat over a purple top, standing in the background with a shocked expression as the transformation unfolds.

Cosmic Title Card.description: A space-themed background with Earth and Mars, displaying bold white text: 'THE LEAGUE AND THE TEAM START UNCOVERING GRANNY GOODNESS'S SECRETS', signaling a narrative shift toward uncovering hidden truths.

Miss Martian.description: A woman with dark hair, wearing a purple and blue armored suit with a star emblem on the chest, glowing with a blue aura, and holding a sword; she exudes confidence and readiness, appearing as a hologram beside the Bald Man in Orange and Green Suit, now seen in a futuristic council chamber.

Holographic Display.description: A futuristic, glowing green hologram in the background, showing a Y-shaped structure, indicating advanced technology in the setting; in this segment, it displays a digital map with a route from Beverly Hills to Encino, passing near the Luther Grande Hotel.

Futuristic Council Chamber.description: A high-tech, purple-lit room with glowing tables and advanced technology, where heroes gather for strategic discussions, emphasizing the League's covert operations; in this segment, it serves as the setting for a meeting with Miss Martian and other heroes.

Goggle Goggles.description: A sleek, black, futuristic head-mounted device with a purple glowing bar, labeled 'goggle goggles', used for augmented reality navigation and data visualization, now being worn by the man in the white t-shirt.

Bald Man in Orange and Green Suit.description: A bald man wearing a form-fitting suit with orange torso and green arms, featuring black accents and a V-shaped emblem on the chest; he appears serious and authoritative, speaking at a podium during a live press conference, announcing the official opening of the first metahuman youth center for children and teens victimized by metahuman trafficking with a clear, authoritative voice.

Young Man with Glowing Red Eye.description: A young man with dark hair, shown putting on the 'goggle goggles', which activate with a purple light, causing his eyes to glow green as he views a digital map highlighting a route from Beverly Hills to Encino, passing near the Luther Grande Hotel, visually demonstrating the goggles' function as explained in the audio.

Man in White T-Shirt.description: A man with brown hair, wearing a plain white t-shirt, shown putting on a futuristic black device labeled 'goggle goggles', and speaking in a clear male voice, explaining the investigation into 'Granny Goodness' and the 'fight club for meta-teen gladiators'.

Digital Map Interface.description: A glowing, purple-outlined digital map displayed through the goggles, showing 'Greater Bialya' and a list of names, indicating real-time data analysis and tracking.

Man in Tuxedo.description: A man with brown hair, wearing a black tuxedo with a bow tie, embracing the man in the armored suit in a dark theater, and speaking in a deep, calm male voice, saying, 'You are free now, my sister. Free and safe.'

Superman T-Shirt Man.description: A man with dark hair wearing a black t-shirt with a red Superman 'S' emblem, standing among the group, observing the exchange with a serious expression.

Galaxy Broadcasting System.description: A live news broadcast with a red and blue graphic overlay, displaying the date 'September 29' and time '11:07 EDT', featuring a studio interview with G. Gordon Godfrey and Gretchen Goode.

Man in Purple and Black Armored Suit.description: A man with dark hair, wearing a purple and black armored suit with a helmet, standing in a futuristic chamber with green-lit pillars, later kneeling under a spotlight, and then embraced by a man in a tuxedo in a dark theater, visually representing a participant in a high-stakes challenge or a moment of freedom, while the audio simultaneously describes the auction of a female 'Tara'.

Greater Bialya Map.description: A purple-hued digital map of a fictional country labeled 'Greater Bialya', displayed on a high-tech interface, with a list of names and a flag, indicating a geopolitical or investigative focus.

Masked Figures in Formal Attire.description: A group of figures wearing white masks and formal suits, holding numbered paddles, standing on a balcony above the man in the armored suit, indicating they are judges or participants in a competitive event.

Blonde Woman in Dark Jacket.description: A woman with blonde hair, wearing a dark jacket over a black top, standing beside the group, watching the interaction with a neutral, observant expression.

Green-Skinned Woman in Purple Cardigan.description: A woman with green skin and brown hair, wearing a purple cardigan over a yellow top, who hands the 'goggle goggles' to the man in the white t-shirt, suggesting she is a key figure in the team's investigation.

Gretchen Goode / 'Granny Goodness'.description: An older woman with gray hair styled in a bun, wearing a dark blazer over a white collared shirt, smiling warmly during the interview, later shown with a sinister, knowing grin, and speaking in a sinister female voice, stating, 'No worries, my friends. A plan is in place.'

G. Gordon Godfrey.description: A man in a suit, seated in a studio, interviewing Gretchen Goode, his expression professional and engaged.

raw_segments: The sequence opens with a close-up of Victor Stone, now partially cybernetic, his face illuminated by intense purple energy crackling across his skin and mechanical arm, indicating a painful transformation. A calm, scientific female voice explains the process, aiming "to purge Fatherbox's soul from the technology contaminating you." A deep, analytical male voice observes, stating he has "never seen a Fatherbox completely override an organic system" and that "such a thing is worthy of study." This is met with an urgent, emotional plea from a Man in a White Lab Coat, who watches Victor's transformation with alarm, exclaiming, "Study it? You need to stop it! My son is dying!" Victor, seated in a high-tech chair within a sterile lab, convulses as blue and purple energy surges through him, accompanied by loud mechanical whirring and energy crackles. A Woman in a White Lab Coat stands beside the concerned Man, covering her mouth in shock. The Man in White Lab Coat, now identified as Victor's father, desperately calls out, "Look at him in the chair! Motherbox, tell the chair to save Vic!" The energy intensifies, Victor’s mechanical arm glows with blue energy, and his chest emits a bright light as he screams in agony, his cries blending with the crescendo of energy sounds. A glowing blue ring forms around his head and chest, culminating in a massive blue explosion that engulfs the lab. As the light fades, Victor’s face is shown with a new, glowing red eye on his forehead and a red circular device on his chest, signifying a complete cybernetic evolution. He groans in pain, but the calm analytical male voice reassures him, "You're stabilized. You'll be all right." The scene ends with a cosmic title card displaying bold white text: 'THE LEAGUE DEVELOPS A CENTER FOR TRAFFICKED META-TEENS TO REHAB,' set against a backdrop of Earth and Mars, suggesting a broader narrative shift toward rehabilitation and societal response to meta-human crises.

The segment opens with an ominous, suspenseful background score as a live broadcast from Galaxy Broadcasting System shows a bald man in an orange and green suit, identified as Aquaman/Justice League Co-Chair, speaking at a podium during a Justice League/S.T.A.R. Labs press conference. He authoritatively announces the official opening of the first metahuman youth center for children and teens victimized by metahuman trafficking. The scene transitions to a cosmic title card revealing the League has discovered a major player in the meta-trafficking ring who may know the location of Brion’s missing sister. A questioning male voice then asks, "What do we know and how do we know it?" as a close-up of a futuristic black device labeled 'goggle goggles' appears. An explanatory male voice details that these VR goggles, manufactured by Gretchen Goods Good World Studios, were designed to test for the metagene, brainwash potential metahumans, and send them to specific 'depot' locations. Visually, a man with gray hair and a bat emblem on his shirt puts on the goggles, followed by a young man with dark hair who also dons the device, which activates with a purple light, causing his eyes to glow green as he views a digital map highlighting a route from Beverly Hills to Encino, passing near the Luther Grande Hotel. The explanatory voice continues, stating the Justice League and team are sending squads to take down all these depots simultaneously. The video then cuts to a futuristic, purple-lit council chamber where several heroes, including Miss Martian, are gathered around a glowing table, with the young man appearing in deep concentration. Another questioning voice mentions, "We got the Bealian gig because of the Tara connection," prompting a third voice to ask, "What is the Tara connection?" The explanatory voice begins to reveal, "Intel from another source. A tip that the Shadows handed Tara over to someone named..." before being cut off as the video cuts to a holographic display showing Victor Stone with a red eye and cybernetic enhancements, experiencing a red energy surge emanating from his chest, indicating a transformation or power activation.

The segment opens in a modern, dimly lit living room where a group of young heroes, including a man in a white t-shirt with a black bat emblem, are gathered. A green-skinned woman in a purple cardigan hands a sleek black device labeled 'goggle goggles' to the man in the white t-shirt, who puts it on. He explains in a clear male voice that 'Granny' was too generic, but now they believe 'Granny' refers to Gretchen Goode's nickname in the popular press: 'Granny Goodness.' The scene cuts to a live broadcast from Galaxy Broadcasting System, featuring G. Gordon Godfrey interviewing Gretchen Goode, also known as 'Granny Goodness,' who is smiling warmly. The man's voice continues, revealing that Granny Goodness's shell company purchased an abandoned mall in 'Greater Bialya,' which is one of the meta-depot locations they hacked off the goggles. The broadcast transitions to a high-tech interface, displayed through the goggles, showing a digital map of 'Greater Bialya' and a list of names, suggesting a data search. The man's voice further explains they found a list on the dark web of merchandise available for purchase at this depot, highlighting 'item 16: female, age 15, Earth powers.' A female voice then exclaims 'Tara!' The man's voice dramatically reveals, 'This isn't just a depot. It's a fight club for meta-teen gladiators,' as intense background music and crowd sounds begin. The focus shifts to a man in a purple and black armored suit with a helmet, standing in a futuristic chamber with green-lit pillars. He is then shown kneeling under a spotlight, surrounded by masked figures in formal attire holding numbered paddles, indicating a game or competition. A booming male auctioneer's voice announces, 'Starting bid for Tara! He's 500,000 US!' and then, 'I see we have our first bid!' A soft, pained female gasp is heard. The scene cuts to a dark theater where the same man in the armored suit is embraced by a man in a tuxedo, who says in a deep, calm voice, 'You are free now, my sister. Free and safe.' Soothing, ethereal music plays. A title card appears: 'THE LEAGUE AND THE TEAM START UNCOVERING GRANNY GOODNESS'S SECRETS,' set against a cosmic backdrop with Earth and Mars. The final shot is a close-up of Granny Goodness, her face illuminated with a sinister, knowing smile, as she states in a sinister female voice, 'No worries, my friends. A plan is in place.'

event_blocks.summary: A menacing figure in a red and black armored suit with a glowing red eye is introduced in a dimly lit chamber, as a female voice declares, "We'll deal with these naughty children in our own good time." In a spaceship, Superman, Wonder Woman, and a green-skinned warrior observe a holographic display, with Superman noting, "Great Scott! Up ahead. We're coming up on some kind of asteroid base. One that's easily twice the size of the Watchtower." A massive, dark spaceship with red glowing engines flies through a nebula, prompting the female voice to shout, "Open fire for Granny! Hail Granny Goodness! Hail Darkseid!" followed by an explosion. The spaceship activates a red energy field, causing Superman and Wonder Woman concern, with Wonder Woman stating, "The children. I think we found them." Superman confirms, "The missing eight from Markovia?" and Wonder Woman clarifies, "No. All of them. The end point of the widespread trafficking of metahumans to the forces of Apokolips." A prison-like facility with glowing orange containment pods is shown, holding silhouetted figures, including a young man with blue skin and a red beanie speaking to a girl with purple eyes. Superman remarks on the spaceship's size, "That may not be the worst of it. Look at the size of this thing. Scans read all of the stolen elements within it." A high-tech chamber with a golden, glowing structure is revealed, surrounded by heroes including a woman in a black and gold suit, who appears to be leading. A text overlay reads, 'After saving many trafficked meta-teens, team members form a new team to instill hope.' At a festive fair, a blue-skinned boy asks about the meta kids, and a boy in a Superman t-shirt expresses a desire to inspire them, while a green-skinned boy notes the difficulty. The narrative then shifts to a different scene where a blonde woman in a brown and white outfit stands near a 'Candy' and 'Cold Drinks' stand, as a male voice states, "Covert thing gets in the way of the inspiration thing. Something is missing." A calm female voice asks, "Okay, I'll bite. What?" In space, a green-skinned young man in a red and white suit, Beast Boy, proposes, "A public version of the team. Young heroes rebelling against the system to fight the good fight in clear view." He is shown with a diverse group of young heroes, including Superboy, a blue-skinned figure, Wonder Girl, and Miss Martian, standing before a large, glowing vertical structure. Beast Boy criticizes the League's limitations, citing headlines about Lex Luthor's influence, anti-meta violence, and metateen trafficking, and names Granny Goodness and Baron Bedlam as capitalizing on these issues. A dark-skinned man with a shaved head and a green and black suit is shown holding a futuristic weapon. Beast Boy confidently asserts their plan to break the League's rules, connect with people, quiet public fears, and inspire the metagene generation, demanding commitment. An older male voice then decides, "So it is decided. The covert team will now consist of Miss Martian, Superboy, Tigress, Halo, 13, Forager, and Terra, while Beast Boy will lead Wonder Girl."

Up ahead. We're coming up on some kind of asteroid base. One that's easily twice the size of the Watchtower,' and later discusses the 'missing eight from Markovia' and the 'stolen elements' within the massive spaceship.

Identified as Beast Boy, he speaks with a deep, authoritative voice, outlining the team's mission to be a public force, fight against global threats, and inspire the metagene generation, concluding with a forceful ultimatum. The scene shifts to a spaceship interior where Superman, exclaiming "Great Scott! Up ahead. We're coming up on some kind of asteroid base. One that's easily twice the size of the Watchtower," Wonder Woman, and a green-skinned warrior with horns observe a holographic display. Hail Darkseid!" followed by a powerful explosion. Man, I wish there were some grand gesture I could make to truly inspire them," and a green-skinned boy whose expression turns serious as he adds, "That's the problem with the team," stand together, looking determined. The video opens with a close-up of a blonde woman with green eyes, wearing a brown and white outfit with a quiver, standing in front of a brightly lit 'Candy' and 'Cold Drinks' stand, as a deep, authoritative male voice states, "Covert thing gets in the way of the inspiration thing. Something is missing." A calm female voice then asks, "Okay, I'll bite. What?" The scene quickly shifts to a dark, star-filled space environment where a green-skinned young man in a red and white suit, later identified as Beast Boy, appears, looking serious and determined. The male voice, belonging to Beast Boy, continues, proposing "A public version of the team. Young heroes rebelling against the system to fight the good fight in clear view." The camera pulls back to reveal Beast Boy with a full group of diverse young heroes standing together in space, including a man in a yellow and red suit with a lightning bolt emblem, a blue-skinned figure in a dark suit, a man in a blue vest with a yellow 'S' emblem (Superboy), a woman in a black and red suit with a yellow lightning bolt (Wonder Girl), a man in a dark green suit, a woman in a white and purple suit (Miss Martian), a woman in a brown and black suit (Tigress), and a large, muscular man in a blue and white armored suit. The group stands in front of a large, glowing vertical structure with blue light strips. Beast Boy's voice criticizes, "The League can't do what we need because you're playing on the Light's game board. Have you seen the headlines lately?" Floating holographic news screens appear, displaying headlines: 'LUTHOR RESTRICTS LEAGUE ACTION PENDING U.N. REVIEW' with a bald man in a suit, 'BREAKING NEWS: ANTI-META RALLY ENDS IN VIOLENCE' showing a crowd, and 'LOCAL TEEN DISAPPEARS: META-TRAFFICKING SUSPECTED' with a young girl’s face. The green-skinned young man gestures toward the screens, his expression shifting from serious to a slight, confident smirk as he details the threats: "Lex Luthor's grip on the United Nations. The public fear of metahumans. Metateen trafficking. Bad guys like Granny Goodness and Baron Bedlam are capitalizing on all of it." The camera then focuses on a close-up of a dark-skinned man with a shaved head and a green and black suit, holding a large, futuristic weapon, looking concerned. The scene returns to the group, now with Beast Boy crossing his arms, standing confidently at the center, flanked by his teammates, including Superboy, the blue-skinned figure, the man in the yellow and red suit, Wonder Girl, and the man in the dark green suit. Beast Boy asserts their plan: "We're going to break the rules the League can't. We're going to connect with people in ways that can't be blocked by governments. We're going to quiet the fears of the public, terrified by the metagene generation, and we'll do it all by inspiring that generation. Get on board or get out of the way!" The atmosphere is tense and focused. A different, calmer, older male voice then states, "So it is decided. The covert team will now consist of Miss Martian, Superboy, Tigress, Halo, 13, Forager, and Terra, while Beast Boy will lead Wonder Girl."

event_blocks.summary: The narrative begins with a tense close-up of the Dark-Skinned Man with Shaved Head and Green and Black Suit, his futuristic weapon poised, as a male voice introduces a new 'public squad' comprising 'Blue Beetle, Kid Flash, Static, and Geo-Force,' who operate covertly under Miss Martian's direction. The scene abruptly transitions to a colossal, dark alien spaceship descending upon a cityscape, unleashing destructive blue energy beams amidst dramatic, ominous music. On a rocky outcrop, a team of heroes—including the Green-Skinned Young Man in Red and White Suit, the Woman in Black and Red Suit with Yellow Lightning Bolt, the Man in Blue Vest with Yellow 'S' Emblem, the Blue-Skinned Figure in Dark Suit, and the Man in Yellow and Red Suit with Lightning Bolt—confronts the threat. A female voice questions their identity, prompting the Green-Skinned Young Man to declare, 'We're something new. Here to help people. We're definitely not playing by the insiders' rules. We can't. We are all outsiders.' A live news broadcast, 'ALIEN INVASION STRIKES BROOKLYN!', shows a panicked reporter exclaiming, 'Oh no! Aquaman's hit!' A cosmic title card then reveals the underlying scheme: 'THE TEAMS LED BY BATMAN & MISS MARTIAN SECRETLY STAGE EVENTS TO MAKE THE OUTSIDERS POPULAR.' Within a high-tech command center, Batman, in his black armored suit, and Miss Martian, in a purple and blue armored suit, observe. Miss Martian explains, 'Aquaman was on scene to throw the fight,' while Batman discusses deploying 'Garfield's squad' and utilizing 'Blue Beetle' against 'Whisper's Reach tech,' also mentioning 'damaged bots' salvaged by 'Robin' to deceive the Outsiders, though acknowledging 'Lex will know this was a setup.' The heroes are shown preparing for action again in a dark, rocky setting before the final scene returns to the command center, where Batman, Miss Martian, and the Woman in White and Purple Suit monitor the operation, underscoring its clandestine nature.

He is identified as 'Blue Beetle' and is considered a 'best bet' against 'Whisper's Reach tech' as part of the new public squad.

He speaks with a deep, authoritative male voice, discussing strategy, robot origins, and the awareness of Lex Luthor regarding their 'setup,' noting that 'knowing and proving are two different things.'

event_blocks.summary: In a high-tech command center, Batman, his expression stern, declares to Diana, "This is a war." A holographic projection of Miss Martian expresses concern over their actions, stating, "We're not simply hiding things anymore. We have gone beyond lying to our comrades and the public. Now we're staging false events, creating fake news? Merely to make our opponents look bad? Or the outsiders and by extension the rest of us look good? Even with the best of intentions, that is crossing a line." Batman, Nightwing, and Oracle, amidst a low technological hum, are shown focused in the command center. In a dimly lit room, Gretchen Goode, an older woman, asks Garth, in his Atlantean suit, and the Man in Teal and Orange Suit, "Cards on the table, boys. Why are you here?" Garth explains two friends visited and disappeared, prompting Gretchen to retort, "You mean two of your friends broke into my home illegally?" The Man in Teal and Orange Suit demands, "Take us to them." Gretchen chillingly reveals, "Those that require correction and discipline are brought here, to my X-Pit." A transparent wall reveals the trio from the outside, with a red targeting box appearing over them, indicating surveillance. The scene shifts to a chaotic, abstract environment with swirling golden and brown hues, where the three are violently thrown through the air by an unseen force, accompanied by whooshing sounds. A large, menacing robot with glowing red eyes and pink energy lines appears, its face filling the screen, uttering in a deep, distorted, menacing voice, "Obey your Granny! My command is Darkseid's will!" The trio tumbles through the chaotic space again, before the red targeting box reappears with a yellow exclamation mark. Garth defiantly states, "We'll show ourselves out," but Gretchen Goode's voice cuts him off, "I said you could take your two friends. I did not say..." The robot's red eyes glare as the scene fades to black.

Her alias 'Granny' is later invoked by the menacing robot.

Command Center.description: A high-tech, dimly lit room with multiple glowing blue screens, holographic displays, and advanced technology, where Batman, Nightwing, and Oracle are gathered, emphasizing the strategic and secretive nature of their mission.

Chaotic Abstract Environment.description: A swirling, golden-brown, amorphous space with particle effects, serving as the battleground where the trio is violently thrown, indicating a supernatural or energy-based attack.

raw_segments: The video opens with a close-up of Batman in a high-tech command center, his expression stern as he speaks in a deep, commanding voice, stating, "This is a war, Diana." Glowing blue screens illuminate the background as the scene cuts to a holographic projection of Miss Martian, her face concerned and illuminated in blue, speaking with urgency: "And which side are we on? We're not simply hiding things anymore. We have gone beyond lying to our comrades and the public. Now we're staging false events, creating fake news? Merely to make our opponents look bad? Or the outsiders and by extension the rest of us look good? Even with the best of intentions, that is crossing a line." The camera pulls back to reveal Batman, Nightwing, and Oracle in a wheelchair, all focused in the command center amidst a low technological hum. An eerie, atmospheric music begins as the scene shifts to a dimly lit room where Gretchen Goode, an older woman with gray hair in a bun, wearing a dark blazer, speaks in a slightly sinister female voice to Garth in his Atlantean suit and the Man in Teal and Orange Suit, asking, "Cards on the table, boys. Why are you here?" Garth, with a direct male voice, responds that two of his friends paid her a visit and disappeared, to which Gretchen retorts, "You mean two of your friends broke into my home illegally?" The Man in Teal and Orange Suit then demands, "Take us to them." Gretchen Goode, with a chilling tone, reveals, "Those that require correction and discipline are brought here, to my X-Pit." A transparent wall then reveals the trio from the outside, as a red targeting box appears over them, indicating surveillance. The scene transitions to a chaotic, abstract environment with swirling golden and brown hues, where the three are violently thrown through the air by an unseen force, accompanied by whooshing sounds. A large, menacing robot with glowing red eyes and pink energy lines appears, its face filling the screen, uttering in a deep, distorted, menacing voice, "Obey your Granny! My command is Darkseid's will!" The trio is shown again, tumbling through the chaotic space, before the red targeting box reappears, now with a yellow exclamation mark, suggesting a critical alert. Garth defiantly states, "We'll show ourselves out," but Gretchen Goode's voice cuts him off, "I said you could take your two friends. I did not say..." The final shot shows the robot again, its red eyes glaring, as the scene fades to black, implying an imminent confrontation.

event_blocks.summary: Gretchen Goode, identified as 'Granny Goodness', sternly declared that someone could take the Mother Box and that such behavior required correction, just before a robotic figure with a glowing green chest and red eyes, now emanating pink energy, distortedly commanded, 'Strike, Overlord! Kill the Mother Box!' Intense energy buildup, explosions, screams, and whooshing sounds ensued as a red energy beam from a floor device struck a wall, causing debris to fly. Garth, in blue and black armor, and another man in a dark suit with orange accents confronted the beam, with Garth concernedly asking, 'You... you feel that?' to which Viloet, in a dark hijab, tensely replied, 'Yes.' Ominous hums and crackling energy intensified as a fiery sphere erupted in the spacious, modern room where heroes, including Victor Stone with a red glowing eye, were gathered. The heroes scattered, with Victor Stone and Viloet leading the retreat, as another explosion rocked the room. A close-up showed a small, rectangular Reach Device with glowing red and blue lights on the floor, emitting crackling blue energy. A man in a dark jacket, 'Kalder', picked it up with concern. Viloet, now glowing with purple energy, urgently exclaimed 'Wait!' and 'Mother Box!' before using the device to project a powerful purple beam engulfing the group, stating with an echoing voice, 'Do not fear, sister. I will heal you now,' while Kalder strained to yell, 'Crush that magic!' The segment concluded with Gretchen Goode showing shock as the robotic figure's distorted voice echoed, 'Annihilate all! Crush them all!'

Reach Device.description: A small, rectangular device with glowing red and blue lights, shown on the floor, which emits a distinct crackling blue energy and is later picked up by the Man in Dark Jacket, triggering Viloet’s power activation.

Robotic Figure.description: A tall, humanoid robot with a green glowing chest and eyes, now menacingly glowing with pink energy lines and red eyes, exuding a hostile presence while speaking in a deep, distorted, commanding voice, ordering to 'Strike, Overlord! Kill the Mother Box!' and later to 'Annihilate all! Crush them all!'

Garth.description: A muscular man with dark hair tied back, wearing a dark blue armored suit with glowing light-blue lines, standing alert and ready to confront the energy beam, speaking with concern as he asks 'You... you feel that?' and later an urgent 'Vikan Buntu?'

Man in Dark Jacket.description: A man with brown hair and a beard, wearing a dark jacket, who is addressed as 'Kalder' and reacts with concern as he picks up the Reach Device, later exclaiming 'Crush that magic!' in a strained voice as Viloet activates her powers.

Victor Stone.description: A muscular young man with short dark hair, now fully cybernetic with red energy lines coursing through his body, a central red eye on his forehead, and a mechanical arm, standing beside Viloet with a serious expression.

Gretchen Goode / 'Granny Goodness'.description: An older woman with gray hair styled in a bun, wearing a dark blazer over a white collared shirt, initially speaking with a stern, commanding voice about the 'Mother Box' and 'correction', later shown with a shocked and wide-eyed look as she observes the energy surge.

Viloet.description: A woman with dark skin wearing a dark hijab, initially appearing concerned and responding 'Yes' to Garth, then urgently exclaiming 'Wait!' and 'Mother Box!', before undergoing a transformation with glowing purple eyes and projecting a powerful purple energy beam, indicating her discovery of new powers linked to Victor Stone’s cybernetic evolution, speaking with a calm, determined, and echoing voice, stating 'Do not fear, sister. I will heal you now.'

Kill the Mother Box!" event_blocks.summary: The Light uses Dr. Helga Jace as a mole to capture Violet, with a deep male voice stating, "The tongue of the old gods." In a lab, Dr. Jace prepares a syringe, telling the voice, "Need not concern yourself with Halo. I already have plans for her capture. That gives me first rights to her." She injects a restrained Viloet, who screams, "No!" and unleashes a purple energy beam, shattering the lab's glass. Dr. Jace declares, "I've fulfilled my part of the bargain. I'm ready to depart with Brion and Tara as promised," but the male voice retorts, "You'll get what you're owed when we are good and ready." In a bright chamber, Viloet, a gorilla-like Man in Fur-Lined Green Outfit, and a white-haired Man in White Lab Coat stand with others. The white-haired man, holding a glowing blue cube, asks, "Would you care to see what we have planned for this Mother Box girl?" Dr. Jace, shocked, admits, "I admit, I'm curious." He commands, "Mother Box, rise," and they are enveloped in white light, transitioning to a swirling, golden-brown abstract space with intense energy sounds. A red targeting box appears, and Dr. Jace exclaims, "Overlord!" They are silhouetted against a fiery red backdrop, and the white-haired man explains, "This is an X-Pit. We're safe within the cube, which protects..." before the audio cuts off. Later, a man with gray hair and red eyes, identified as 'matron,' explains the 'deleterious' effects of the 'Pit's ghost dimension' on 'mind, spirit, and body' to a concerned woman with glasses. A group, including a gorilla-like figure with glowing red eyes and cybernetic enhancements, and a blindfolded woman in a dark hijab, are trapped in a red cube above an apocalyptic landscape. The 'matron' reveals his methods of 'discipline, correction, and reprogramming' are 'flawed and impermanent,' as 'free will has not been truly eliminated.' The blindfolded woman screams, reacting to the 'Goose Dimension' repolarizing the 'Mother Box's human's' power, as a figure in a purple and blue armored suit appears. A sinister female voice explains a woman is "the key to solving the anti-life equation," defined as "life minus free will equals anti-life." The blindfolded woman, Halo, screams violently, enveloped in a purple energy aura, then is shown fearful inside a red containment cube. Her eyes glow purple, and she projects an energy beam, shattering the cube. The female voice confirms Halo is connected to the "source of all life" without Metron's safeguards, making her the key to the anti-life equation. Dr. Helga Jace states, 'Plus Halo equals anti-life,' and asks, 'Do you have truths you wish to share with your children, doctor?' A woman in a white lab coat with glasses, channeling purple and golden energy, is addressed by Dr. Jace, who claims, 'Tara and Brion are mine! My creations!' A young man with green hair and glowing red eyes shows a transformation. In a lab, Dr. Jace, holding a glowing blue cube before a containment pod with a blonde woman, recounts destroying the old Tara and birthing her as a metahuman, declaring, 'Tara was reborn as a metahuman. A metahuman I had brought into this world. She was now my daughter!' The blonde woman breaks free, restrained by the Man in Red and Gold Armored Vest. Dr. Jace vows revenge on Baron Bedlam and to forge a new weapon. Dr. Jace, glowing with purple energy, says, 'Brion is such...' A calm female voice narrates manipulating a young man into becoming a meta, his eyes glowing red, stating, "my son into this world." In a dimly lit room, a man in a dark coat restrains a fearful woman in a beige hooded outfit, observed by the narrator, Dr. Jace. She introduces the woman as "Gabrielle," initially deemed "useless" for lacking the meta-gene. A fiery figure erupts outdoors. Back in the lab, the narrator injects Gabrielle, explaining she "put her to sleep with an overdose of morphine," but Gabrielle survived and became a meta-human. In a surreal environment, Gabrielle's eyes glow purple as she unleashes energy. The narrator concludes Gabrielle has been "reborn as my daughter Violet." Dr. Helga Jace narrates, "and Brion had been reborn as my son." She admits seducing Jeff to reunite with her children. Learning about Gabrielle and the Motherbox spirit, she suspected Violet wasn't her creation. Testing Violet's hair follicles, she discovered Gabrielle never had the meta-gene, calling the girl "a corpse revivified by an alien machine." The woman's eyes glow purple as she unleashes an energy blast, declaring, "And worse, this thing was dating my son! I had to separate them." The scene shows the woman in the dark hijab's shock and a young man with glowing red eyes. Dr. Jace, engulfed in purple energy, details her plan: "So I lied, telling her she was dying. Even convincing her not to burden her friends with her illness. Anything to keep her dependent on me and drive a wedge between this pretender and my boy. But I knew this subterfuge..."

Dr. Helga Jace.description: A woman with brown hair tied in a ponytail, wearing glasses and a white lab coat, initially calm and focused, then shown with intense, glowing eyes as she unleashes a powerful purple energy blast, her expression shifting from concern to determination. She narrates in a calm yet intense and manipulative voice, revealing her plan to separate her son from 'Gabrielle' by seducing 'Jeff,' testing 'Violet's' hair follicles, and lying to 'Gabrielle' about her impending death to keep her dependent and drive a wedge between her and Jace's son.

The deep male voice continues, instructing, "Need not concern yourself with Halo. I already have plans for her capture. That gives me first rights to her." Dr. Jace approaches a restrained Viloet, who is lying on a medical table, wearing a dark hijab and a green top. Dr. Jace's voice is heard saying, "I see. Overlord. Cerebral leash, please." Dr. Jace injects Viloet, who then suddenly activates a powerful purple energy beam, causing a surge that shatters the lab’s glass walls, accompanied by Viloet's pained scream of "No!" and the sound of breaking glass. Dr. Jace's voice is heard stating, "I've fulfilled my part of the bargain. I'm ready to depart with Brion and Tara as promised." The deep male voice replies with authority, "You'll get what you're owed when we are good and ready." The scene cuts to a brightly lit chamber where Viloet, the large gorilla-like Man in Fur-Lined Green Outfit, the white-haired Man in White Lab Coat, and two others stand. The white-haired man, holding a glowing blue cube emitting golden light, asks with a confident, almost smug expression, "Would you care to see what we have planned for this Mother Box girl?" Dr. Jace, looking on with shock and concern, admits, "I admit, I'm curious." The white-haired man then commands, "Excellent. Mother Box, rise." The group is then enveloped in a blinding white light, transitioning into a swirling, golden-brown abstract space with intense energy sounds and a high-pitched whine. A red targeting box with a yellow exclamation mark appears over them, indicating a critical threat, as Dr. Jace exclaims, "Overlord!" The scene ends with the group silhouetted against a fiery red backdrop, their expressions tense and determined. Dr. Jace asks, "What... what is this?" The white-haired man, speaking with authority, explains, "This is an X-Pit. We're safe within the cube, which protects..." before the audio cuts off.

The video opens with a close-up of a man with gray hair, wearing a black suit and white shirt, his face illuminated by a red-orange glow, suggesting a tense and dangerous environment. He speaks with a calm, authoritative, and almost smug expression, explaining the 'deleterious' effects of the 'Pit's ghost dimension' on 'mind, spirit, and body.' Beside him, a woman with glasses and dark hair looks on with concern. The camera then reveals a group of five individuals, including a large, muscular gorilla-like figure with glowing red eyes and cybernetic enhancements, wearing a red and gold armored vest, standing protectively beside a blindfolded woman in a dark hijab. They are all trapped inside a transparent red cube floating above a fiery, apocalyptic landscape. The man in the suit, who identifies his role as 'matron' in honing 'young warriors' and using 'expits' for 'discipline, correction, and reprogramming,' gestures toward the blindfolded woman. He confidently states his methods are 'quite effective in the short term,' but reveals his 'dirty little secret': the process is 'flawed and impermanent,' as 'free will has not been truly eliminated.' Close-ups show the blindfolded woman's intense fear, the woman with glasses' concern, and the gorilla-like figure's anger, emphasizing their emotional states. The man continues, explaining he 'formulated a theory' after seeing a 'mother box trapped inside this organic form.' Suddenly, the blindfolded woman emits a loud, high-pitched scream, followed by a sustained, almost electronic whine, as if reacting to his words or the environment. The man in the suit, now standing beside the glaring gorilla-like figure, calmly dismisses her reaction, saying, 'You see? It's a reflex. She can't help herself.' He attributes this to the 'Goose Dimension' having 'repolarized the power emanating from the Mother Box's human,' as a figure in a glowing purple and blue armored suit appears in the background, adding to the high-stakes confrontation unfolding amidst the swirling smoke and red-orange light.

The video opens with a close-up of a man in a black suit with gray hair and red eyes, standing beside a large, gorilla-like figure with cybernetic enhancements and glowing red eyes, both bathed in an ominous red-orange glow. The scene cuts to a woman in a dark hijab, blindfolded with a purple band, who is suddenly enveloped in a purple energy aura, screaming violently as she is thrown backward through a chaotic, golden-brown abstract environment. With a powerful surge, the woman's eyes glow purple, and she projects an energy beam, shattering the cube amidst an explosion of debris and a distorted, almost demonic voice. The man in the suit watches with a delighted, almost mocking smile, while the gorilla-like figure glares with anger, asking, "What just happened?" The female voice explains this as "another proof," confirming the woman, referred to as "Halo," is connected to the "source of all life" but without Metron's technological safeguards. The gorilla-like figure concludes that "Halo's organics make her the key to this anti-life equation," a statement the female voice affirms, believing her connection to the source of life is "repolarized." The scene concludes with the woman floating, still blindfolded, as the man in the suit and the gorilla-like figure stand together, their faces illuminated by the red glow, in a tense, apocalyptic atmosphere charged with supernatural energy.

The segment opens with a close-up of a man with gray hair and red eyes, wearing a black suit and black earrings, standing beside a large, gorilla-like figure with cybernetic enhancements and glowing red eyes, both bathed in an ominous red-orange glow. Though the man visually speaks with a smug, confident expression, the audio features Dr. Helga Jace's calm, declarative voice stating, 'Plus Halo equals anti-life,' followed by 'But one final test remains. Do you have truths you wish to share with your children, doctor?' The scene cuts to a woman in a white lab coat with glasses, her face illuminated by swirling purple and golden energy, suggesting she is channeling or reacting to a powerful force. I...' before Dr. Jace's voice, now slightly condescending, interjects, 'Don't be modest, dear. Spill your secrets.' The distressed voice responds, 'I... I only wanted to protect my Kinder!' Dr. Jace's voice then becomes possessive and triumphant, declaring, 'But Tara and Brion are mine! My creations!' The scene then cuts to a young man with green hair and glowing red eyes, his face tense and focused, indicating a transformation or power activation. I destroyed the old Tara. Drowned her.' She proudly states, 'What emerged was not the Markazian human daughter. No. Tara was reborn as a metahuman. A metahuman I had brought into this world. The final shot returns to Dr. Jace, now glowing with purple energy, her eyes wide and intense, as if she is being overwhelmed or activated by the power she is channeling, concluding with her saying, 'Brion is such...' The video opens with a close-up of a young man, his face illuminated by a warm, golden light, as a calm, authoritative female voice narrates how she manipulated this "sweet boy" into becoming a meta. His eyes then glow fiercely red against a fiery background, signifying his transformation, which the narrator proudly states was her act of bringing "my son into this world." The scene shifts to a dimly lit room where a man in a dark coat restrains a fearful woman in a beige hooded outfit, while the woman in a white lab coat (the narrator) observes. In a surreal, glowing environment, Gabrielle's eyes glow purple as she unleashes energy, while the lab technician appears in a translucent, ethereal form, her expression shifting from concern to determination. The narrator concludes, believing Gabrielle has been "reborn as my daughter Violet," as the lab technician's face is illuminated by golden light, her eyes glowing with power in a sunlit, dreamlike landscape, resolute and focused, all set to a tense, dramatic orchestral score.

The minute opens with a close-up of Dr. Helga Jace, her face illuminated by a warm, golden light, her expression tense and concerned, as she narrates in a calm yet intense voice, "and Brion had been reborn as my son." A subtle, ominous musical score begins to build. The scene quickly shifts to a dimly lit bedroom where she is seen lying beside a man, her hand gently resting on his chest, suggesting a moment of intimacy or care, as she continues, "But how was I to stay near you both, let alone find Tara? So I seduced Jeff. Used him to reunite with my children." The setting then transitions to a suburban house at dusk, where a group of young heroes, including a blonde woman in a dark jacket and a woman in a dark hijab, stand outside, engaged in a serious conversation. A corpse revivified by an alien machine." The tension escalates as a hand with pink-painted nails holds a pair of glasses, which are then placed on her face, triggering a surge of purple energy that envelops her. Her eyes glow intensely as she unleashes a powerful energy blast, her face contorted in effort, declaring, "And worse, this thing was dating my son! I had to separate them." The scene cuts to the woman in the dark hijab, her expression shifting from concern to shock, followed by a close-up of a young man with glowing red eyes, his face illuminated by a fiery red light, indicating a transformation or power activation. The sequence concludes with Dr. Jace, now fully engulfed in purple energy, her hands raised as if channeling or controlling the power, her expression one of intense focus and determination, as she details her manipulative plan: "So I lied, telling her she was dying. Even convincing her not to burden her friends with her illness. Anything to keep her dependent on me and drive a wedge between this pretender and my boy. But I knew this subterfuge..."

event_blocks.summary: Dr. Helga Jace, glowing with intense purple energy, expresses her determination to 'create more' after being reunited with her children, recounting her arrangement with Ultra-Humanite for an 'exit' and a place to 'continue my work, increasing the size of our family,' while apologizing for 'control chips' due to fear of losing them again, assuring they'll 'no longer be necessary' with a sinister chuckle. event_blocks.summary: In a red-lit chamber, a scarred man with a red collar and a hooded figure with glowing red eyes stand near the Man in Golden and Orange Armored Suit (Darkseid) and a Man in Dark Suit with White Collar, as Granny Goodness states, “You'll find her a very well-behaved child,” and Darkseid replies, “Just as I prefer.” A cosmic title card declares 'VANDAL SAVAGE ENLISTS THE LEAGUE TO SABOTAGE GRANNY AND DARKSEID'S PLANS.' Vandal Savage provides coordinates for a Mother Box to “boom tube to Miss Harper's location,” and when asked “What's the catch?”, he instructs, “You promise to tell her abductor exactly who sent you.” A group of heroes, including a Superman T-Shirt Man and a Woman in Purple and Blue Armored Suit, gather in a high-tech facility, where a Young Man with Glowing Tablet observes a bald man in a purple and blue suit with a red 'X' emblem, who explains they are “almost certainly aboard Granny Goodness's asteroid base” and that “The League was staging a raid here today to liberate dozens of kidnapped metateens.” Heroes in a blue energy sphere fly through a nebula, while a large Armored Figure with Glowing Chest and Red Eyes is seen in a green energy field, followed by a high-pitched, distorted female scream and an explosion. The heroes, led by the bald man, are then in a purple energy sphere against a cosmic background. Back in the red-lit chamber, Darkseid addresses Granny Goodness, who gleefully declares, “What a delightful test of the anti-life equation. The Justice League's biggest bullies enslaved in one fell swoop. Now, the children... Mother Box, your friends certainly...” before the audio cuts off. The scene then shifts to a dim, red-lit chamber where heroes, including a Young Man in Gold and Black Suit, an Orange-Skinned Warrior, and a Blonde Woman in Form-Fitting Suit, are confronted by the Man in Golden and Orange Armored Suit, who plans to 'brain blast everyone.' As a hero shouts, 'Let Viloet go!', the blindfolded Woman in Beige Hooded Outfit (Viloet) stands beside the armored man. A sinister female voice describes how 'non-meta' populations will 'suffer from the process' and die, finding it 'truly glorious to contemplate.' The Man in Golden and Orange Armored Suit roars in rage as a red energy wave surges, causing heroes to clutch their heads amidst groans and screams, with one pleading, 'Please, hurry!' and another directing, 'Target that device.' Viloet unleashes a powerful purple energy beam, her eyes glowing intensely, accompanied by loud energy discharge sounds and explosions, as the chamber is bathed in red light.
