raw_segments: The minute opens dramatically with a red-tinted close-up of Granny Goodness, her face contorted in rage, as she delivers a sinister, cackling monologue, declaring, "Well, at long last. Rise, children, and bear witness. The age of the Anti-Life Equation is finally upon us. The age of Darkseid begins!" She looms over distressed heroes, including the Bald Man in Purple and Blue Suit with Red 'X' and the Man in Red Suit with Lightning Bolt. The scene then shifts to a cosmic view of a massive, purple energy sphere crackling with lightning, accompanied by an ominous electronic hum, while a text overlay emphasizes, 'VICTOR AND THE OUTSIDERS ARE THE ONLY HEROES LEFT TO STOP GRANNY GOODNESS.' The focus transitions to Victor Stone, now fully cybernetic with a glowing red eye, standing in a high-tech environment. With a calm, analytical voice, he explains his ability to access electronic information, stating, "I can reach out with my mind to anything on the web, or the cloud, or well, anything electronic, really." He activates a holographic display, revealing a red-outlined target: "Building 16," a storage facility with "no network connectivity," where he found "a clear Apokoliptan signature." Victor then declares, "There," as the scene cuts to the dimly lit industrial warehouse. Here, Victor and the Outsiders—including the Woman in Black and Red Suit with Yellow Lightning Bolt, the Man in Yellow and Red Suit with Lightning Bolt, the Blue-Skinned Figure in Dark Suit, the Green-Skinned Young Man in Red and White Suit, and the Man in Blue Vest with Yellow 'S' Emblem—gather around a massive, red-eyed mechanical device. The device emits a loud, ominous whirring and a distinct, low, guttural hum. The Man in Yellow and Red Suit with Lightning Bolt speaks urgently, describing it as "A big sci-fi machine making a loud, nefarious..." before the machine's sound cuts him off. Victor, with his mechanical arm, stands resolute amidst the tense green and red lighting, confidently stating, "Give me a second. I can figure it out. I speak its language." The Man in Yellow and Red Suit with Lightning Bolt responds with surprise, "You speak..." followed by the machine's guttural sound, as the team prepares for confrontation.

event_blocks.summary: In a dimly lit, red-tinged industrial warehouse, the Green-Skinned Young Man in Red and White Suit, the Man in Yellow and Red Suit with Lightning Bolt, the Woman in Black and Red Suit with Yellow Lightning Bolt, and the Man in Blue Vest with Yellow 'S' Emblem stand alert amidst intense electronic sounds and whooshes. An explosion violently throws the Man in Blue Vest backward, eliciting a pained grunt, while the Man in Yellow and Red Suit shouts, "Come on! Come on!". The scene transitions to a neon-blue digital environment where a cybernetic Victor Stone, with red energy lines and a central red eye, clutches his chest in pain, uttering "Oh" and "Double wall" as a towering robotic figure with pink energy lines looms behind him. A strategic male voice, identified as the Man in Yellow and Red Suit, states, "The old Vic can't beat this bruiser. So I better sub the new Vic into the game." Victor activates his powers, his mechanical arm glowing with red energy, and unleashes a pink energy blast that engulfs the robot in a massive energy surge. The narrator exclaims, "Now we're talking! You're the true source of Granny's power, aren't you? You're the techno-cancer she's using to hurt my friends. Well, guess who brought the techno-cure!" Back in the warehouse, Gretchen Goode / 'Granny Goodness' stands illuminated by green and red light, furiously shouting, "Overlord! Who told you to end the lesson? Overlord!" The heroes regroup, the Woman in Black and Red Suit holding a golden lasso, as the Green-Skinned Young Man calmly retorts, "Lesson one. Don't come to the party," with lingering electronic hum and energy sounds.

A massive explosion then erupted, bathing the scene in fiery orange and yellow light, revealing Granny Goodness, her face contorted in rage as she unleashed a powerful energy blast, her furious screams of "No! No! No!" echoing with the roaring energy. The chamber, now bathed in red light, showed the energy beam striking the ground and creating a fiery vortex, prompting the urgent male voice to state, "If I hurry, I can follow... She'll lead me right to Violet." A loud, distorted roar and a powerful energy surge accompanied this, leading the male voice to reflect, "I knew it. That machine kept Granny separated in two." The scene shifted to a close-up of Granny Goodness, her face illuminated by red lightning and contorted in fury, before transitioning to a surreal, pink-lit space where Viloet, a woman in a hijab with glowing purple eyes, appeared, projecting an energy beam and calmly explaining, "Yes. I remember. I've been repolarized. If I simply act on my instincts, I'll continue to enslave everyone to the Anti-Life Equation. But I can't leave any of them like this. And I believe there is a way." She was joined by a muscular man in a purple and black armored suit, and as the energy intensified, forming a rainbow-colored vortex that engulfed the chamber, a younger male voice, likely Victor Stone, remarked, "Rainbow power. Cool." The event culminated in a cosmic view of a massive golden structure descending through a vibrant nebula, surrounded by planets and stars, underscored by intense, dramatic orchestral music.

He speaks with a younger, slightly impressed tone, remarking 'Rainbow power. Cool.'

Granny Goodness.description: A large, imposing woman with dark hair and a stern, angry expression, shown in a red-tinted close-up, her face glowing with rage, suggesting she is the primary antagonist. She screams 'No! No! No!' in distress or anger, and is later mentioned by another character as 'Granny' who was 'separated in two' by a machine.

Viloet.description: A woman with dark skin wearing a dark hijab and a beige hooded outfit, blindfolded with a glowing purple band over her eyes, her expression shifting from fear to intense focus as she unleashes a powerful purple energy beam. She speaks with a calm, ethereal female voice, explaining her 'repolarized' state and her struggle against the 'Anti-Life Equation,' stating her determination to find a way. She is also referred to by name by another character.

Man in Purple and Black Armored Suit.description: A man with dark hair, wearing a purple and black armored suit with a helmet, standing in a futuristic chamber. He speaks with a determined and concerned male voice, asking 'You with me?', stating his intent to 'follow' to 'Violet,' and reflecting on 'Granny' being 'separated in two.'

Golden Central Structure.description: A large, glowing golden structure at the center of a high-tech chamber, radiating energy and serving as a focal point for the heroes' gathering.

raw_segments: The sequence opens in a dimly lit, green-hued chamber where Victor Stone, a young man with a glowing red eye, stands beside a blue-skinned figure, confronting a looming threat. A determined male voice asks, "You with me?" to which another replies, "Absolutely!" as intense electronic sounds build. Suddenly, a massive explosion erupts, engulfing the scene in fiery orange and yellow light, revealing Granny Goodness, an imposing woman whose face is contorted in rage as she unleashes a powerful energy blast. Her furious screams of "No! No! No!" echo amidst the roaring energy. The camera cuts to a wide shot of the chamber, now bathed in red light, as the energy beam strikes the ground, creating a fiery vortex. The determined male voice, now urgent, states, "If I hurry, I can follow... She'll lead me right to Violet." A loud, distorted roar accompanies a powerful energy surge. The male voice then reflects, "I knew it. That machine kept Granny separated in two." The scene shifts to a close-up of Granny Goodness, her face illuminated by red lightning, screaming in fury. The environment then transitions to a surreal, pink-lit space where Viloet, a woman in a hijab with glowing purple eyes, appears, projecting a beam of energy. A calm, ethereal female voice, likely Viloet, explains, "Yes. I remember. I've been repolarized. If I simply act on my instincts, I'll continue to enslave everyone to the Anti-Life Equation. But I can't leave any of them like this. And I believe there is a way." She is joined by a muscular man in a purple and black armored suit. As the energy intensifies, forming a rainbow-colored vortex that engulfs the chamber, a younger male voice, likely Victor Stone, remarks, "Rainbow power. Cool." The scene culminates in a cosmic view, with a massive golden structure descending through a vibrant nebula, surrounded by planets and stars, accompanied by intense, dramatic orchestral music, suggesting a grand, interstellar confrontation is unfolding.

event_blocks.summary: A purple hooded figure with glowing purple eyes descends through a multicolored vortex into a dark chamber, eliciting a roar and a plea of "Tanya, don't!" followed by an impact. The scene shifts to a fiery landscape where a colossal dark-skinned figure with red eyes looms over a kneeling armored warrior amidst sounds of struggle and grunts. The Green-Skinned Young Man in Red and White Suit reveals they were sent by Vandal Savage, prompting a roar and energy discharge. In a futuristic council chamber, the Green-Skinned Young Man observes a holographic display of heroes, including the Woman in Purple and Blue Armored Suit, who confesses to Lord Darkseid that Vandal Savage sabotaged her anti-life equation implementation, leading to an energy discharge and explosion. Later, in a dimly lit command center, Superman T-Shirt Man expresses relief at Victor Stone's arrival, with the Green-Skinned Young Man and the Woman in Dark Hijab affirming their return, and the latter announcing the League's imminent return with rescued meta-humans. In a separate event, Garfield Logan, the Green-Skinned Young Man, welcomes Victor Stone to the Outsiders, who accepts his new role. A Galaxy Broadcasting System report by Tod Donner reveals a meta-human has joined Beast Boy's squad, identifying the new Outsider as Cyborg (Victor Stone) and showing Gretchen Goode under suspicion for meta-trafficking, with Donner reporting her alleged alien nature. A title card announces 'THE LIGHT START A PROXY WAR IN MARKOVIA, RELYING ON THEIR OWN MOLE, TERRA MARKOV,' followed by Donner reporting Baron Bedlam's coup in Markovburg. In space, Wonder Girl and a man in a red suit observe a holographic Lex Luthor, with Wonder Girl explaining Luthor has sidelined the League and manipulated public opinion, and that a win for Brion and the Outsiders would aid the League's cause.

Colossal Dark-Skinned Figure.description: A massive, dark-skinned humanoid with glowing red eyes and a stern, menacing expression, standing in a fiery, apocalyptic landscape, towering over a kneeling warrior.

Figure in Dark Blue Hooded Cloak.description: A figure in a dark blue hooded cloak with a red emblem, standing among the heroes, partially obscured, observing the situation with a stoic demeanor.

Woman in Dark Hijab.description: A woman with dark skin wearing a dark hijab, initially seen in a serious conversation outside a suburban house, her expression shifting to shock and concern as she witnesses Dr. Jace's power activation. She has a calm, affirming female voice.

Futuristic Council Chamber.description: A high-tech, purple-lit room with glowing tables and advanced technology, where heroes gather for strategic discussions, emphasizing the League's covert operations; in this segment, it serves as the setting for a meeting with Miss Martian and other heroes.

Superman T-Shirt Man.description: A young man with dark hair, wearing a black t-shirt with a red Superman 'S' emblem, standing among his teammates in a high-tech facility, observing with a serious expression. He has an excited, relieved male voice, and calls the Green-Skinned Young Man 'Vic' and 'Violent.'

Fiery Red-Veined Figure.description: A humanoid figure engulfed in red flames with glowing red veins, suggesting a powerful, possibly corrupted or transformed state, which emerges from the ground and is later defeated.

Young Man with Glowing Red Eye.description: A young man with dark hair, shown in close-up, whose eyes glow red, indicating a transformation or power activation, his expression fierce and determined, set against a fiery red background.

Red-Lit Chamber.description: A dimly lit, angular chamber with red lighting and stone-like architecture, serving as a villain’s lair or command center, where key confrontations occur.

Green-Skinned Young Man in Red and White.description: Garfield Logan / Beast Boy, a young man with green skin, black hair, and green eyes, wearing a red and white sleeveless suit, who confidently asks for votes and leads the 'Outsiders'.

Kneeling Armored Warrior.description: A muscular figure in red armor with a horned helmet and glowing yellow eyes, crouched in a ready stance, then confronting the Skull-Masked Figure face-to-face before turning to look up at the descending structure with defiance.

Heroes in Purple Energy Sphere.description: A group of heroes in a purple energy sphere, floating in a cosmic background, led by the Bald Man in Purple and Blue Suit, indicating a strategic or protective formation.

Cosmic Nebula Environment.description: A vast, colorful nebula with swirling pink, purple, and blue clouds, dotted with stars and planets, serving as the backdrop for the descent of the golden structure, indicating a transition to an interstellar scale.

Woman in Purple and Blue Armored Suit.description: A woman with dark hair, wearing a purple and blue armored suit with a star emblem on the chest, glowing with a blue aura, appearing as a hologram or distant figure, possibly an ally or observer. She has a remorseful female voice, addresses 'Lord Darkseid,' and reveals that 'Vandal Savage' sabotaged her work, including the 'anti-life equation.'

Holographic Display.description: A futuristic, glowing green hologram in the background, showing a Y-shaped structure, indicating advanced technology in the setting; in this segment, it displays a digital map with a route from Beverly Hills to Encino, passing near the Luther Grande Hotel, now replaced by a red-outlined target of a container marked '16'.

Baron Bedlam.description: A large, stone-faced man with gray skin, wearing a red military-style uniform, identified by Tod Donner as Frederick De Lamb, who staged a coup in Markovia.

Man in Red Suit with Lightning Bolt.description: A man in a red suit with a yellow lightning bolt emblem, shown in a red-tinted scene with a serious expression, appearing overwhelmed by Granny Goodness's presence.

Woman in Black and Red Suit with Yellow.description: A blonde woman with green eyes, wearing a black top with a yellow lightning bolt emblem and red pants, standing with one hand on her hip, exuding confidence and strength.

Galaxy Broadcasting System.description: A live news broadcast channel, featuring anchor Tod Donner, reporting on meta-human activities and political events.

Tod Donner.description: A man in a dark suit and tie, serving as the professional news anchor for Galaxy Broadcasting System, reporting live from New York.

White-Skinned Woman with Red Eyes.description: A woman with white skin, red eyes, and a red and black armored suit, standing with a stern expression, her face illuminated by red light, appearing as a powerful and menacing figure.

Blonde Woman in Dark Jacket.description: Wonder Girl / Cassie Sandsmark, a woman with blonde hair, wearing a dark jacket, who observes a holographic display and explains the political machinations involving Lex Luthor and the League.

Lex Luthor.description: A bald man in a suit, identified as U.N. Secretary General, who is described as having sidelined the League and manipulated public opinion.

Victor Stone.description: Victor Stone / Cyborg, a muscular young man, now fully cybernetic with a central red eye, who acknowledges his new status as an 'outsider' with a serious, determined expression.

Gretchen Goode / 'Granny Goodness'.description: An older woman with gray hair and red eyes, identified by news anchor Tod Donner as a 'media mogul' and 'alien entity' under suspicion for deep space meta-trafficking.

raw_segments: The minute opens with a vibrant, cosmic explosion of light and color, accompanied by intense, dramatic orchestral music that builds to a powerful crescendo. A deep, guttural roar or scream erupts as a female figure in a purple hooded outfit, her eyes glowing with intense purple energy, descends through a radiant, multicolored vortex. She lands in a dark, angular chamber where a group of heroes, including Superman T-Shirt Man and the Green-Skinned Young Man in Red and White Suit, stand in awe or concern. Amidst the continuing roar, a female voice shouts "Tanya, don't!", echoed by a male voice, followed by a sound of impact. The scene dramatically shifts to a fiery, apocalyptic landscape under a massive, red-hued sky, where a colossal, dark-skinned figure with glowing red eyes looms menacingly over a kneeling, armored warrior. Sounds of struggle, grunts, and a powerful "Ugh!" from a male voice accompany this intense visual, underscored by more intense orchestral music and a final grunt. A calm, male voice, belonging to the Green-Skinned Young Man in Red and White Suit, then states, "If you're wondering how we found you, you can thank Vandal Savage. He sent us and wanted you to know." This is followed by a powerful, almost animalistic roar or scream, accompanied by a sound of energy discharge. The narrative then cuts to a futuristic council chamber, where the Green-Skinned Young Man in Red and White Suit sits on a red couch, observing a holographic projection of the heroes, including the Woman in Purple and Blue Armored Suit, as they appear in a glowing, colorful formation. A female voice, identified as the Woman in Purple and Blue Armored Suit, remorsefully states, "I failed you, Lord Darkseid. But all my works in your name, including the implementation of the anti-life equation, were sabotaged by none other than Vandal Savage." This revelation is punctuated by an intense sound of energy discharge and impact, resembling a powerful explosion. The final frames show the heroes in a dimly lit, high-tech command center, with the Woman in Dark Hijab and the Figure in Dark Blue Hooded Cloak standing among them, their expressions serious and focused, as they confront an unseen threat. A male voice, identified as Superman T-Shirt Man, exclaims with relief, "Vic! Violent! I knew you'd make it!", followed by a chuckle. The Green-Skinned Young Man in Red and White Suit responds, "Good to be back." The Woman in Dark Hijab affirms, "Indeed it is." Another female voice then shares more good news: "The League will soon return to Earth with hundreds of rescued meta-humans."

The scene opens in a modern command center where Garfield Logan, a green-skinned young man in a red and white suit, smiles slightly as he asks, "All in favor of Vic joining the outsiders?" A chorus of 'I!' is heard, followed by Victor Stone, a cybernetic man with a red glowing eye, stating, "I guess I'm an outsider," amidst cheers. The view widens to show a group of heroes, including a white-skinned woman with red eyes, gathered around a central table. The focus shifts to a live news broadcast from Galaxy Broadcasting System, with anchor Tod Donner reporting from New York. Donner's professional voice announces, "Yet another meta-human has joined Beast Boy's rebel squad of young heroes, known as Cyborg. The newest Outsider has already made a splash..." The broadcast shows Victor Stone with the headline 'CYBORG JOINS OUTSIDERS,' then cuts to a red-tinted image of Gretchen Goode, an older woman with white hair and red eyes, screaming, under the headline 'GRETCHEN GOODE UNDER SUSPICION.' Donner continues, "...by releasing footage that places the blame for this deep space meta-trafficking squarely on the back of media mogul Gretchen Good. Claiming that the much-beloved Granny Goodness is actually an alien entity," as dramatic, suspenseful music swells. The scene returns to the command center, where Garfield Logan and a woman in a dark hijab watch the news with concern. A cosmic title card appears, stating 'THE LIGHT START A PROXY WAR IN MARKOVIA, RELYING ON THEIR OWN MOLE, TERRA MARKOV.' The broadcast then shows pre-recorded footage from Markovburg, Markovia, featuring a large, stone-faced man, Baron Bedlam, with the headline 'BEDLAM STAGES COUP.' Donner identifies him as "Frederick De Lamb, also known as Baron Bedlam," who "escaped from a Markovian prison and staged a coup." The scene transitions to space, showing the Watchtower orbiting Earth, then to a dark chamber where Wonder Girl, a blonde woman in a dark jacket, and a man in a red suit with a lightning bolt observe a holographic display of Lex Luthor. Wonder Girl's voice explains, "Look, as you've probably guessed, the League's been sidelined by UN Secretary General Lex Luthor. And frankly, by public opinion, which Luthor's manipulated. Both sides know a win for Brion and the Outsiders will also help the League's cause." The final shot is a close-up of Wonder Girl, arms crossed, and the stern-looking man, both focused on the display.

event_blocks.summary: In a high-tech Futuristic Council Chamber, Miss Martian, identified as M'gann, announced that bio-ships were crossing the Vladovin border into Markovian airspace and that she was leading Gamma Squad to prevent Dr. X from executing loyalists to King Gregor Marko, while Beta Squad prepared to boom tube to Markovburg to support Alpha Squad. Simultaneously, in Hollywood, masked operatives, including a blonde woman with arrows and a man in a black mask, prepared for action, and on a nighttime cliffside, Victor Stone, a winged figure, and a dark-skinned man in a green suit stood ready. Lex Luthor, a bald man in a suit, projected a holographic map of Markovburg castle, declaring that all heroes and metahumans must be registered and regulated worldwide, asserting that his own team would volunteer and Infinity Incorporated would supplant the Justice League. A man with white hair and an eyepatch warned that the Light was filling its ranks with controlled metahumans, rendering current heroes outlaws subject to arrest and inhibitor collars, and pondered how to guarantee a debacle. In a secure channel, the man with white hair and an eyepatch instructed Deathstroke to publicly kill Garfield Logan, while a stone-like creature with glowing red eyes roared in a graveyard, confronted by the Man in Green and Black Suit, who generated a fiery energy sphere, and the Man in Red Cap and Tactical Vest. The Man in Green and Black Suit yelled about his sister, parents, and betrayal, as a distressed female voice cried out, "Geo-Force, no!" The Man in Green and Black Suit, identified as Geo-Force, stood over the defeated creature, while Tara, the blonde woman in the dark jacket, appeared tense. Deathstroke commanded Tara to kill Beast Boy, but an urgent female voice pleaded with her not to, and the Man in Red Cap questioned how she knew about Deathstroke. The man with white hair and an eyepatch interjected, surprised the channel was not secure. Tara resolutely raised her hand as a reassuring female voice offered forgiveness, but a slicing sound followed. Lex Luthor and the man with white hair and an eyepatch faced a holographic projection of Baron Bedlam, with Luthor addressing him as "Mr. Secretary." A cosmic title card revealed that "BRION IS BESTED BY HIS RAGE AND MURDERS BEDLAM, AND IS KICKED OFF OF THE OUTSIDERS."

Masked Figures in Black Tactical Gear.description: Two figures in black hooded tactical suits with red emblems, standing in a dimly lit industrial setting, suggesting they are part of a covert team, now seen in a dark, tense environment.

Miss Martian.description: A woman with dark hair, identified as M'gann, wearing a purple and blue armored suit with a star emblem on the chest, glowing with a blue aura, standing in a high-tech command center, appearing focused and authoritative as she speaks about 'a loss,' 'bio-ships crossing the Vladovin border into Markovian airspace,' and her role 'leading Gamma Squad' to prevent Dr. X from executing loyalists, also mentioning 'Beta Squad is preparing to boom tube straight to Markovburg to back up Alpha Squad.'

Nighttime Cliffside.description: A grassy cliff under a starry sky, where three heroes stand in a defensive stance, ready for confrontation, with a dark, natural atmosphere and a sense of impending battle.

Container Marked '16'.description: A red-outlined target on a digital map, labeled '16', shown on a holographic display in the command center, indicating a specific objective or threat.

Man with White Hair and Eyepatch.description: A man with white hair and an eyepatch, wearing a black tactical suit with brown shoulder armor and dual katanas, standing in a dark, outdoor setting beside the Bald Man in Dark Suit, observing a red-lit hologram of Baron Bedlam, and speaking with an authoritative male voice, stating 'I thought this channel was secure.'

Batman.description: A figure in a black armored suit with a bat emblem, standing with arms crossed, displaying a stern and commanding presence, now shown in a close-up with a serious expression in a high-tech command center.

Futuristic Council Chamber.description: A high-tech, purple-lit room with glowing tables and advanced technology, where heroes gather for strategic discussions, emphasizing the League's covert operations; in this segment, it serves as the setting for a meeting with Miss Martian and other heroes.

Command Center.description: A high-tech, dimly lit room with multiple glowing blue screens, holographic displays, and advanced technology, where Batman, Nightwing, and Oracle are gathered, emphasizing the strategic and secretive nature of their mission.

Nightwing.description: A young man in a black tactical suit with blue accents and a mask, displaying intense focus and using a high-tech gauntlet to scan a device in a warehouse; his blue eyes are sharp and determined, now standing beside Batman and Oracle in the command center.

Hollywood Setting.description: A modern, industrial-style facility with a large circular portal, dim lighting, and a group of masked operatives preparing for action, indicating a covert operation or mission briefing.

Blonde Woman in Dark Jacket.description: A woman with blonde hair, wearing a dark jacket, identified as 'Tara' by urgent female voices, appearing concerned and tense, her expression shifting to resolute as she raises her hand, seemingly under the influence of 'Deathstroke' but also being offered forgiveness by a calm female voice.

Graveyard Scene.description: A dark, eerie graveyard with bare trees and a group of masked figures in black suits with red glowing eyes digging a grave, suggesting a ritualistic or sinister event, with a cold, blue-toned lighting.

Lex Luthor.description: A bald man in a suit, standing in a high-tech room, projecting a holographic map of a castle, his expression serious and commanding, indicating he is orchestrating a strategic operation. He speaks with a deep, authoritative voice, declaring that 'this will be a complete debacle for the outsiders,' and asserting that 'all heroes and metahumans must be registered and regulated, worldwide,' further stating his 'own team of young champions will quickly volunteer to register, and Infinity Incorporated will supplant the Justice League.'

Victor Stone.description: A muscular young man with short dark hair, fully cybernetic with red energy lines coursing through his body, a central red eye on his forehead, and a mechanical arm, standing with a serious, determined expression in a high-tech environment.

Holographic Gauntlet Interface.description: A futuristic, glowing interface projected from Nightwing’s gauntlet, displaying scientific data, chemical analysis, and a national flag, indicating advanced technology used for investigation, now part of the command center's interface.

Holographic Map of Markovburg Castle.description: A glowing blue holographic projection of a fortified castle on an island, with a red dot indicating a target, displayed on the floor in a high-tech room, used for strategic planning.

Holographic Display.description: A futuristic, glowing blue hologram showing multiple figures, including a woman with a golden headpiece (possibly 'Tara' mentioned in the briefing), a man with a red eye, and another with a white face and red eyes, indicating a surveillance or briefing interface for the assassination target 'Garfield Logan'.

Hooded Archer.description: A muscular man in a green hooded outfit with arrows on his back, initially seen observing from a forest, then using a high-tech interface to scan targets, and later being struck by a fiery energy whip, appearing injured, now standing among the operatives in Hollywood.

Man in Green and Black Suit.description: A young man with brown hair and a headband, wearing a dark green tactical suit with black accents, generating a fiery energy sphere in his hands during a confrontation in a graveyard.

Man in Red Cap and Tactical Vest.description: A man with a red cap and tactical vest, holding a communication headset, standing beside the Man in Green and Black Suit during the confrontation with the stone creature, and later expressing surprise with a male voice, asking 'Deathstroke? How does she...'

Bald Man in Dark Suit.description: A bald man in a dark suit and tie, standing beside the Man with White Hair and Eyepatch, observing a red-lit holographic projection of Baron Bedlam, and speaking with a calm, sinister male voice, addressing an unseen party as 'Mr. Secretary' after discovering their presence on the line.

Stone-Like Creature.description: A large, monstrous figure with a rocky, gray body, glowing red eyes, and sharp teeth, roaring aggressively in a graveyard setting before being defeated with a powerful impact sound.

Green-Skinned Young Man in Red and White.description: A young man with green skin, black hair, and green eyes, wearing a red and white sleeveless suit with a high collar, standing in a stone archway with a serious, observant expression, later shown standing over the defeated stone creature, and identified as 'Beast Boy' by a commanding male voice.

Baron Bedlam.description: A large, stone-faced man with gray skin, wearing a red military-style uniform, shown as a red-lit holographic projection with a castle image below, and associated with a deep, commanding male voice, identified as 'Deathstroke,' who states 'Bedlam caused all your misery' and commands 'Kill Beast Boy. Now.'

Cosmic Title Card.description: A space-themed background with Earth and Mars, displaying bold white text: 'BRION IS BESTED BY HIS RAGE AND MURDERS BEDLAM, AND IS KICKED OFF OF THE OUTSIDERS,' signaling a major plot point.

raw_segments: The minute opens with a sweeping aerial view of Markovburg at night, illuminated by city lights and a dramatic sky, featuring a bridge leading to a fortified island and a large castle-like structure. The scene cuts to the high-tech Futuristic Council Chamber where Miss Martian, identified as M'gann, in her purple and blue armored suit with a star emblem, stands at the center, flanked by other heroes. She speaks with a focused, authoritative tone about 'a loss' and 'bio-ships crossing the Vladovin border into Markovian airspace,' explaining that she's 'leading Gamma Squad' to prevent Dr. X from executing those loyal to King Gregor Marko, adding that 'Beta Squad is preparing to boom tube straight to Markovburg to back up Alpha Squad.' The visual then shifts to a dark graveyard where masked figures in black suits with red glowing eyes are digging a grave, suggesting a sinister ritual. Next, the setting changes to Hollywood, where a group of masked operatives, including a blonde woman with a quiver of arrows and a man in a black mask, are preparing for action. The scene transitions to a nighttime cliffside where three heroes—Victor Stone (cybernetic with red energy lines), a winged figure, and a dark-skinned man in a green suit—stand ready for confrontation. A bald man in a suit, Lex Luthor, appears in a high-tech room, projecting a holographic map of the Markovburg castle with a red dot indicating a target. He speaks with a deep, authoritative voice, declaring that 'this will be a complete debacle for the outsiders,' asserting that 'all heroes and metahumans must be registered and regulated, worldwide.' He continues, stating his 'own team of young champions will quickly volunteer to register, and Infinity Incorporated will supplant the Justice League.' He is joined by a man with white hair and an eyepatch, who intensely adds that 'the Light fills its ranks with metahumans under our control,' and warns that 'the current heroes will be rendered outlaws, subject to arrest, imprisonment, even inhibitor collars,' before pondering 'how to guarantee today's debacle.' The hologram then shows a group of heroes, including Miss Martian, in a blue energy field, suggesting teleportation. The final scenes show Batman and Nightwing in a command center, observing a holographic display of a container marked '16' on a digital map, while a stern-faced man with long dark hair appears on a screen, indicating a high-stakes confrontation is imminent. The environment shifts from urban to high-tech to natural, with lighting ranging from dark and moody to bright and digital, emphasizing the escalating tension and global scale of the conflict, all underscored by the strategic dialogue.

The segment opens in a high-tech command center with a cool blue ambiance, where a man with white hair and an eyepatch, wearing a black tactical suit, stands beside a bald man in a dark suit. Tense, low-humming music underscores their observation of a holographic display, which shows multiple figures including a woman with a golden headpiece. A deep, authoritative male voice, likely the man with the eyepatch, states, "Her cover will be blown, no doubt. We believe the outcome worth the sacrifice. Any objections?" He then confirms, "Channel is secure, proceed. Deathstroke to Tara." The voice continues, revealing a sinister plot: "The moment you've been waiting for is here. On our signal, you are to publicly kill Garfield Logan." The scene cuts to a nighttime outdoor setting, where three heroes stand on a floating rock over water. A close-up shows a young man with white hair and a black suit with a determined expression. The scene shifts to a graveyard where a large, stone-like creature with glowing red eyes roars aggressively amidst sounds of combat and energy blasts. A hero in a green and black suit with a headband confronts the creature, generating a fiery energy sphere. He is joined by a man in a red cap and tactical vest. An aggressive voice, possibly the creature, snarls, "All equally pathetic!" The fiery sphere is unleashed, engulfing the creature in flames. The Man in Green and Black Suit passionately yells, "You kidnapped my sister! Murdered my parents! Betrayed crown and country!" A distressed female voice cries out, "Geo-Force, no!" as the final shot reveals a green-skinned young man, identified as Geo-Force, in a white and red suit, standing in a stone archway with a serious expression. Another calm, authoritative female voice concludes, "The outsiders are beacons of hope. We don't deal out punishment."

The sequence opens in a dark, eerie graveyard at night, accompanied by suspenseful, low-humming music. A confrontation unfolds as the Man in Green and Black Suit generates a fiery energy sphere, facing a large, roaring Stone-Like Creature with glowing red eyes. Nearby, the Man in Red Cap and Tactical Vest observes, while a deep, commanding male voice, identified as Deathstroke, ominously declares, "Bedlam caused all your misery. Do your job, then nothing can stop you and Brion from executing Galam," before commanding, "Kill Beast Boy. Now." A powerful impact sound follows. The scene shifts to a wide shot where the Green-Skinned Young Man in Red and White Suit, identified as "Beast Boy," stands over the now defeated stone creature, with statues and a starry sky in the background. The focus then moves to the Blonde Woman in Dark Jacket, identified as "Tara," who appears concerned and tense. An urgent female voice asks, "Tara, what are you doing?" as Deathstroke's voice reiterates, "Do it. Kill him." The urgent female voice pleads, "Tara, listen. You don't have to do what Deathstroke says," prompting a surprised male voice, likely the Man in Red Cap, to exclaim, "Deathstroke? How does she..." An authoritative male voice, likely the Man with White Hair and Eyepatch, interjects, "I thought this channel was secure." Tara's expression shifts to resolute as she raises her hand, while a calm, reassuring female voice tells her, "You can be forgiven, Tara. The choice is yours." Building suspenseful music accompanies these moments, leading to a loud impact. The urgent female voice cries out again, "Damn it, Tara, listen!" followed by a slicing sound. The camera then shows the Man with White Hair and Eyepatch and the Bald Man in Dark Suit, who stands beside him, both facing a large holographic projection of Baron Bedlam’s face, displayed in red light with a castle image below. A calm, sinister male voice, likely the Bald Man, states, "Well, we wondered who else would be on the other end of this line. Now we know. Mr. Secretary." The final frame is a cosmic title card with Earth and Mars in the background, displaying white text: 'BRION IS BESTED BY HIS RAGE AND MURDERS BEDLAM, AND IS KICKED OFF OF THE OUTSIDERS,' indicating a pivotal narrative development.

event_blocks.summary: In a nighttime outdoor setting, a man in a dark suit and glasses accuses, "Prince Brion brought down the tyrant the lamp. Who staged the coup after escaping prison? After murdering our king and queen." Subsequently, a bearded man in a light blue shirt and suspenders is hailed as 'Hail Brion the King!' by a cheering crowd, including a woman with red hair and a man in a green headband. Brion defends his actions, stating, "I had to do it. Don't you see?" but the man in the suit sternly retorts, "You crossed a line we don't cross. You murdered that man." A bald man in a dark suit analyzes the situation: "Grayson's people in their masks, a coup on live television, an outsider executing an enemy and declaring himself king?" He then muses, "I can easily frame the argument now for heroes to be registered and regulated." The scene shifts to a nighttime cliffside with heroes, including a man in a green and black suit and a large armored figure, in formation, then to a high-tech command center where the bald man and a man with white hair and an eyepatch watch a Galaxy Broadcasting System news report. A voice identified as "The Light" declares, "The Light is satisfied," noting "some legal issues to resolve" but deeming "the rest of the news is good." A cosmic title card appears: 'THE LIGHT, DARKSEID, AND METRON FORMULATE THEIR NEXT PLANS.' The video then shows a snowy mountain range in Western Mongolia, followed by a Black Lightning news report from New York with the headline 'LIGHTNING AUTHOR IS META-TRAFFICKER.' The final scene is a high-tech chamber where the bald man faces a group, including a woman with blonde hair and a man with a white face and red eyes, all observing the unfolding events.

Bearded Man in Light Blue Shirt.description: A man with a brown beard and mustache, wearing a light blue shirt and dark suspenders, raising his arm triumphantly with a determined expression, who states, "I had to do it. Don't you see?"

Woman with Red Hair.description: A woman with long red hair, wearing a gray tank top, smiling and raising her arm in celebration alongside others.

You murdered that man."

Bald Man in Dark Suit.description: A bald man in a dark suit and tie, standing in a high-tech command center, gesturing as he speaks while observing a holographic news broadcast, and whose calm, analytical voice observes, "Grayson's people in their masks, a coup on live television, an outsider executing an enemy and declaring himself king?" before adding, "I can easily frame the argument now for heroes to be registered and regulated."

Cosmic Title Card.description: A space-themed background with Earth and Mars, displaying bold white text: 'THE LIGHT, DARKSEID, AND METRON FORMULATE THEIR NEXT PLANS,' signaling a major plot development.

raw_segments: The video opens with a fiery explosion in a nighttime outdoor setting, as a man in a dark suit and glasses shouts, accusing, "Prince Brion brought down the tyrant the lamp. Who staged the coup after escaping prison? After murdering our king and queen." The scene shifts to a bearded man in a light blue shirt and suspenders, raising his arm triumphantly as the text 'Hail Brion the King!' appears, followed by a cheering crowd, including a woman with red hair and a man in a green headband, who echo the chant, "Hail Brion, the King!" A voice then tells Brion, "Only you have the strength to rule Markovia with the full support of the military," to which Brion responds, "I had to do it. Don't you see?" The man in the suit from earlier then sternly states, "You crossed a line we don't cross. You murdered that man." The mood turns tense as the man in the suit and the man in the green headband look on with serious expressions. A calm, analytical voice, likely the bald man in the dark suit, observes, "Grayson's people in their masks, a coup on live television, an outsider executing an enemy and declaring himself king?" before adding, "I can easily frame the argument now for heroes to be registered and regulated." The scene transitions to a wide shot of a nighttime cliffside where a group of heroes, including a man in a green and black suit and a large armored figure, stand in formation. The setting then shifts to a high-tech command center where the bald man in a dark suit and a man with white hair and an eyepatch observe a holographic news broadcast from the Galaxy Broadcasting System, showing a pre-recorded report dated February 16. A deep, resonant voice, identified as "The Light," declares, "The Light is satisfied," acknowledging "some legal issues to resolve" but concluding that "the rest of the news is good." The bald man gestures as he speaks, and the scene cuts to a cosmic title card reading 'THE LIGHT, DARKSEID, AND METRON FORMULATE THEIR NEXT PLANS' against a backdrop of Earth and Mars. The video then shows a snowy mountain range in Western Mongolia, followed by a live news broadcast from New York featuring a man with a beard and glasses, identified as 'Black Lightning,' with the headline 'LIGHTNING AUTHOR IS META-TRAFFICKER.' The final scene is a high-tech chamber where the bald man in a dark suit stands before a curved window, facing a group of figures including a woman with blonde hair and a man with a white face and red eyes, all observing the situation with serious expressions.

event_blocks.summary: Against a backdrop of dramatic, ominous music, King Viktor, seated on his golden throne, listens as Viyard, a man in a dark suit and glasses, explains that King Brion (likely Viktor) leads a puppet kingdom influenced by a new member with limited metapsychic ability to nudge people toward their worst impulses. In a futuristic command center, Viyard appears on a glowing blue holographic screen and is commended by a deep, authoritative male voice for his services. This scarred man, wearing a dark blue coat, stands in the command center, glaring at the holographic display showing multiple figures, and reveals his recent trip to Apokolips where he and Darkseid reconfirmed their partnership, with Darkseid still searching for the Anti-Life Equation. He emphasizes keeping eyes on Halo, believing she is key to their final victory. The scene shifts to a cosmic setting with a red nebula backdrop, where a massive, armored figure with glowing red eyes, implied to be Darkseid, stands beside the scarred man. A close-up reveals Darkseid's fierce expression before he suspends a smaller figure in mid-air and strikes it with red lightning, while the scarred man watches grimly. Returning to the command center, the scarred man stands before the holographic display now showing Halo and a bald man in a suit. The video then transitions to dark, starry space where a hooded figure in a dark cloak faces a holographic projection of a woman in a dark hijab, with on-screen text reading 'THE WATCHTOWER FEBRUARY 24, 19:00 EST'. Finally, a woman in a purple and blue armored suit, glowing with a blue aura, sits in a high-tech chair as an explosion erupts behind her, defiantly shouting, “Stay back!” before a male voice retorts, “I think not.” A close-up reveals her face, illuminated with a blue energy ring and her eyes glowing orange as she looks determinedly forward.

King Viktor.description: A bald man with a beard, wearing a red military-style uniform with gold epaulets and a blue sash, seated on a golden throne, his expression stern and authoritative, who is referred to as 'King Brion' in the audio, leading a 'puppet kingdom'.

Man in Dark Suit and Glasses.description: A man with short dark hair and glasses, wearing a dark suit and tie, appearing on a glowing blue holographic screen with a tense and serious expression, identified by a deeper voice as 'Viyard', who speaks with a calm, slightly sinister tone about his 'metapsychic ability' to 'nudge people toward their own worst impulses'.

Woman in Purple and Blue Armored Suit.description: A woman with dark hair, wearing a purple and blue armored suit with a star emblem on the chest, glowing with a blue aura, appearing in a high-tech chair amidst an explosion, her face illuminated with a blue energy ring and eyes glowing orange with determination, who shouts 'Stay back!' with a defiant female voice.

Man with Long Dark Hair and Scarred Face.description: A muscular man with long dark hair and a scarred face, wearing a dark blue coat with a red collar, standing in a futuristic command center with a grim, intense expression, who speaks with a deep, authoritative voice, commending Viyard and discussing his recent trip to 'Apokolips' and his 'partnership' with 'Darkseid' regarding the 'Anti-Life Equation', and the importance of 'Halo'.

Text Overlay.description: A white text overlay on a space background reading 'THE WATCHTOWER FEBRUARY 24, 19:00 EST', providing narrative context and a timestamp.

Holographic Display.description: A futuristic, glowing blue hologram showing multiple figures, including a bald man in a suit and a woman with a golden headpiece, which the Scarred Man refers to as showing 'Halo', whom he believes 'could be the key to our final victory'.

Armored Figure with Glowing Red Eyes.description: A massive, armored figure with a dark, rocky body and glowing red eyes, standing in a cosmic red nebula, exuding power and menace, strongly implied to be 'Darkseid' through the Scarred Man's dialogue, and shown striking a smaller figure with red lightning, with a male voice, possibly his, saying 'I think not' in response to the armored woman.

Hooded Figure in Dark Cloak.description: A figure in a dark hooded cloak, standing with their back to the camera, facing a holographic projection of a woman in a dark hijab, in a starry space environment.

raw_segments: Against a backdrop of dramatic, ominous music, the video opens with a close-up of King Viktor, a bald man with a beard in a red military-style uniform, seated sternly on his golden throne, listening intently as a man in a dark suit and glasses, later identified as Viyard, explains in a calm, slightly sinister tone how “King Brion” (likely Viktor) now leads a “puppet kingdom” thanks to “our newest member’s influence” and his “limited metapsychic ability” to “nudge people toward their own worst impulses.” The scene cuts to a futuristic command center where Viyard appears on a glowing blue holographic screen, his face tense and serious, as a deeper, authoritative male voice commends him, stating, “No need to be modest, Viyard. Your services are much appreciated.” The focus shifts to a muscular man with long dark hair and a scarred face, wearing a dark blue coat, who stands in the command center, glaring intensely at the holographic display showing multiple figures, including a bald man in a suit and a woman with a golden headpiece. This scarred man, speaking with the deep, authoritative voice, then reveals his recent trip to “Apokolips,” where he and “Darkseid” “rattled sabers” but ultimately “reconfirmed their partnership,” noting Darkseid’s continued search for the “Anti-Life Equation.” He emphasizes the need to “keep eyes on this Halo,” believing “she could be the key to our final victory.” The scene transitions to a cosmic setting with a red nebula backdrop, where a massive, armored figure with glowing red eyes and a dark, rocky body, strongly implied to be Darkseid, stands beside the scarred man, who turns to face him. A close-up reveals Darkseid's fierce expression before a dramatic moment unfolds: a smaller figure is suspended in mid-air, being struck by red lightning emanating from Darkseid's hands, while the scarred man watches with a grim expression as Darkseid turns away. The scene returns to the command center, where the scarred man stands before the holographic display, now showing a group of figures including the woman with a golden headpiece (Halo) and a bald man in a suit. The video then shifts to a dark, starry space environment, where a hooded figure in a dark cloak stands with their back to the camera, facing a holographic projection of a woman in a dark hijab, with on-screen text reading 'THE WATCHTOWER FEBRUARY 24, 19:00 EST'. The final scenes show a woman in a purple and blue armored suit, glowing with a blue aura, sitting in a high-tech chair as an explosion erupts behind her. She defiantly shouts, “Stay back!” before a male voice, possibly the Armored Figure, retorts, “I think not.” A close-up reveals her face, now illuminated with a blue energy ring, her eyes glowing orange as she looks determinedly forward.

event_blocks.summary: Victor Stone, a cybernetic figure with red energy lines and a red eye, sits in a glowing blue throne within a fiery cavern, clutching his chest with a strained expression as a deep, synthesized, echoing voice, accompanied by mechanical whirring, addresses him as 'grandson' and 'Cyborg,' praising his survival and technological evolution, and identifying him and 'Halo' as vital to an upcoming galactic conflict against 'Darkseid.' As the camera pans out, revealing his full armored form, a young woman in a purple jacket and blue shorts, a young woman in a dark hooded jacket, and a young man in a white hoodie with a mechanical arm urgently run towards him through the lava-filled environment, while the voice continues to express satisfaction with their development and reiterates the threat of Darkseid. A text overlay, 'THE LEAGUE AND TEAM RECUPERATE AFTER LEARNING THE SECRETS THEY KEPT FROM EACH OTHER,' appears against a cosmic backdrop. The scene transitions to a high-tech, blue-lit corridor where Nightwing, in his black and blue tactical suit, stands with a stern, determined expression beside Batman and a man in a blue and black suit with a yellow lightning bolt emblem. Nightwing declares in a clear, determined voice that he, Batman, Wonder Woman, Aquaman, Miss Martian, Oracle, and Robin have been operating a clandestine task force to counter 'the Light,' a secret they withheld with the misguided intention of protecting others.

Nightwing.description: A young man in a black tactical suit with blue accents and a mask, displaying intense focus, standing in a high-tech, blue-lit corridor with a stern expression, his white eyes glowing under the mask, who speaks with a clear, determined voice, revealing that he, Batman, Wonder Woman, Aquaman, Miss Martian, Oracle, and Robin have been running a secret task force to combat 'the Light', which they kept hidden to protect others.

raw_segments: The video opens with a close-up of Victor Stone, a cybernetic figure with red energy lines and a red eye on his forehead, seated in a glowing blue throne-like chair, clutching his chest with a strained expression in a fiery, cavernous environment. A deep, synthesized, echoing voice, accompanied by mechanical whirring, addresses him as 'grandson' and 'Cyborg,' praising his survival and evolution of technology, and declaring him and 'Halo' as crucial players in an impending galactic conflict against 'Darkseid.' As the camera pulls back to reveal his full armored form, three young heroes—a woman in a purple jacket, a woman in a dark hooded jacket, and a man in a white hoodie with a mechanical arm—run urgently toward him through the lava-filled landscape, while the voice continues to express enjoyment in their evolution and warns of Darkseid's threat. A text overlay, 'THE LEAGUE AND TEAM RECUPERATE AFTER LEARNING THE SECRETS THEY KEPT FROM EACH OTHER,' appears against a cosmic background. The scene then shifts to a high-tech, blue-lit corridor where Nightwing, in his black and blue tactical suit, stands with a stern, determined expression alongside Batman and a man in a blue and black suit with a yellow lightning bolt emblem. Nightwing speaks in a clear, determined voice, revealing that he, Batman, Wonder Woman, Aquaman, Miss Martian, Oracle, and Robin have been operating a secret task force to combat 'the Light,' a secret they maintained in a misguided attempt to protect others.

event_blocks.summary: In a high-tech, blue-lit corridor, Nightwing and Batman observe as Black Lightning, sporting a beard and orange-tinted glasses, passionately declares that their organization is a place to learn out of the spotlight, but otherwise, like the Outsiders, they must live in each other's spotlights, asserting that if they fail while doing right, they will have gone down swinging on their own terms and will not fail in the long term, as holding firm to their principles will guide them to an honorable victory. Simultaneously, in a starry space environment, a bald man in an orange and green suit and Wonder Woman, clad in red and gold armor, announce their resignations as co-chair of the League, with Miss Martian, appearing as a blue holographic projection, also stepping down as leader of the team. Back in the corridor, Black Lightning is nominated and elected as the new leader, vowing to Batman and Nightwing that they will not keep secrets from each other and that the ends will not justify the means, refusing to sink to the methods of their enemies while maintaining a covert team for their young heroes, who are later shown as a group of three—a blonde woman, a hooded archer, and a dark-skinned woman with purple glowing lines—standing seriously in space. The segment is underscored by a serious, dramatic orchestral score that swells to an epic and prominent level.

raw_segments: The segment opens with Nightwing, stern and focused in his black tactical suit, standing in a high-tech, blue-lit corridor, as a deep, serious male voice reflects on past 'successes, failures, compromises, deceptions, and betrayals.' The scene then shifts to a starry space environment where a bald man in an orange and green suit, speaking with a deep, authoritative voice, acknowledges, 'we made a mistake,' standing beside Wonder Woman, who wears a red and gold armored suit. Wonder Woman then speaks with a firm, resolute voice, declaring her resignation 'as co-chair of the League, effective immediately,' a sentiment echoed by the bald man. Miss Martian, glowing with a blue aura as a hologram, is heard with a calm, slightly regretful female voice stating she's 'stepping down as leader of the team as well.' The focus returns to the blue-lit corridor, now showing Batman beside Nightwing, both looking intense. Black Lightning, a man with a beard and orange-tinted glasses, enters the frame, speaking with an energetic, authoritative voice, proposing the need for 'a new leader' and then nominating 'Black Lightning.' A sharp female voice quickly seconds the nomination, followed by a chorus of 'Aye!' votes from unseen 'current leaguers' and those 'aboard the Javelin.' Black Lightning, now seen with Batman and Nightwing behind him, continues to speak with commanding authority over a holographic display, emphasizing that 'we will not keep secrets from each other' and 'the ends will not justify the means,' vowing not to 'sink to using the methods of our enemies.' He concludes by stating they will keep their 'covert team' for their 'young heroes.' The final shot reveals a group of three young heroes—a blonde woman, a hooded archer, and a dark-skinned woman with purple glowing lines—standing seriously in space, embodying the 'young heroes' Black Lightning refers to. Throughout the segment, a serious, dramatic orchestral score underscores the gravity of the discussions.

The segment opens with a close-up of Black Lightning, a dark-skinned man with a beard and orange-tinted glasses, wearing a blue and black suit with a yellow lightning bolt emblem, speaking with a serious and intense expression in a high-tech, blue-lit corridor. He passionately declares, "is a place to learn out of the spotlight. But otherwise, like the outsiders, we must live in each other's spotlights." Behind him, Batman, in his iconic black armored suit, and Nightwing, in his black tactical suit, stand stoically, observing. The scene then cuts to a different group: a green-skinned young man in a red and white sleeveless suit with a high collar, holding a green medical brace on his arm, stands beside a young man in a yellow and red suit with a red lightning bolt emblem and red goggles, who smiles confidently with hands on hips. A blonde woman in a black and red suit with a yellow lightning bolt emblem is partially visible to the left. The camera returns to Black Lightning, who continues speaking with growing intensity, his mouth open mid-sentence, stating, "Ah, if we fail while doing right, then at least we went down swinging on our own terms. But in the long term, we will not fail. Holding firm to our principles will guide us to an honorable victory. Now let's get down to business." His resolute voice is accompanied by a dramatic, heroic musical score that swells as he speaks. Batman and Nightwing remain impassive in the background, maintaining the cool, blue, futuristic ambiance. The segment concludes with the music becoming more epic and prominent before a purple screen displays the 'HBO Max' logo in white, with copyright text below.
